kids.question: What was "in the beginning" according to John chapter 1?
kids.explanation: John 1:1 says "In the beginning was the Word."

kids.question: What did John the Baptist call Jesus when he saw Him?
kids.explanation: John 1:29 says "Behold the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world."

kids.question: Which disciple did Andrew bring to Jesus?
kids.explanation: John 1:41-42 tells us Andrew brought his brother Simon Peter to Jesus.

teens.question: According to John 1:1, what is the relationship between the Word and God?
teens.option_a: The Word was God
teens.option_b: The Word was an angel
teens.option_c: The Word was created by God
teens.explanation: John 1:1 states "the Word was with God, and the Word was God."

teens.question: What role did John the Baptist say he played?
teens.option_a: The voice of one crying in the wilderness
teens.option_b: The prophet foretold by Moses
teens.option_c: The messenger of the temple
teens.explanation: John 1:23 — John said "I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness."

teens.question: What new name did Jesus give to Simon?
teens.option_a: Cephas (Peter)
teens.option_b: Barnabas
teens.option_c: Israel
teens.explanation: John 1:42 says Jesus called him "Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone."

teens.question: What convinced Nathanael that Jesus was the Son of God?
teens.option_a: Jesus saw him under the fig tree before Philip called him
teens.option_b: Jesus healed his sickness
teens.option_c: Jesus quoted Scripture to him
teens.explanation: John 1:48-49 — Jesus saw Nathanael under the fig tree, and Nathanael believed.

teens.question: What did Jesus promise Nathanael he would see?
teens.option_a: Heaven open and angels ascending and descending upon the Son of man
teens.option_b: The temple destroyed and rebuilt
teens.option_c: The dead raised to life
teens.explanation: John 1:51 — "Ye shall see heaven open, and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of man."

adults.question: In John 1:3, what does the text say was made without the Word?
adults.option_a: Not any thing that was made
adults.option_b: Only heavenly things
adults.option_c: Only living creatures
adults.option_d: Only mankind
adults.explanation: John 1:3 says "without him was not any thing made that was made."

adults.question: What specific title does John 1:18 use for Jesus in relation to the Father?
adults.option_a: The only begotten Son, which is in the bosom of the Father
adults.option_b: The firstborn of all creation
adults.option_c: The beloved Son in whom I am well pleased
adults.option_d: The express image of His person
adults.explanation: John 1:18 — "the only begotten Son, which is in the bosom of the Father, he hath declared him."

adults.question: According to John 1:12-13, those who received Jesus were born of what?
adults.option_a: God — not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man
adults.option_b: Water and the Spirit
adults.option_c: The word of truth
adults.option_d: Incorruptible seed
adults.explanation: John 1:13 — "born, not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God."

adults.question: What did John the Baptist explicitly deny being when questioned by the priests and Levites?
adults.option_a: The Christ, Elijah, and "that prophet"
adults.option_b: A Pharisee, a Sadducee, and a scribe
adults.option_c: Moses, Elijah, and David
adults.option_d: A priest, a king, and a prophet
adults.explanation: John 1:20-21 — He denied being the Christ, Elijah, or "that prophet."

adults.question: Where was John baptizing when he identified Jesus as the Lamb of God?
adults.option_a: Bethabara beyond Jordan
adults.option_b: The Jordan near Jericho
adults.option_c: The Sea of Galilee
adults.option_d: The pool of Siloam
adults.explanation: John 1:28 — "These things were done in Bethabara beyond Jordan, where John was baptizing."

adults.question: What sign did God give John the Baptist to identify the Messiah?
adults.option_a: The one upon whom the Spirit descends and remains
adults.option_b: A voice from heaven
adults.option_c: A star appearing in the east
adults.option_d: Fire falling from the sky
adults.explanation: John 1:33 — "Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending, and remaining on him, the same is he."

adults.question: What was the approximate hour when the two disciples of John first stayed with Jesus?
adults.option_a: The tenth hour (about 4 PM)
adults.option_b: The sixth hour (about noon)
adults.option_c: The third hour (about 9 AM)
adults.option_d: The ninth hour (about 3 PM)
adults.explanation: John 1:39 — "It was about the tenth hour."

adults.question: What did Philip tell Nathanael about where Jesus was from, and what was Nathanael's response?
adults.option_a: Nazareth; "Can there any good thing come out of Nazareth?"
adults.option_b: Bethlehem; "Is not this the city of David?"
adults.option_c: Capernaum; "That is far from the temple."
adults.option_d: Jerusalem; "Surely the Messiah comes from Judah."
adults.explanation: John 1:46 — Nathanael said "Can there any good thing come out of Nazareth?"

kids.question: What did Jesus turn into wine at the wedding?
kids.explanation: John 2:9 tells us Jesus turned water into wine at the wedding in Cana.

kids.question: Where was the wedding that Jesus attended?
kids.explanation: John 2:1 says "there was a marriage in Cana of Galilee."

kids.question: What did Jesus do in the temple that upset the merchants?
kids.option_a: He drove them out with a whip and overturned their tables
kids.option_b: He closed the doors on them
kids.option_c: He refused to buy their animals
kids.option_d: He told the priests to fire them
kids.explanation: John 2:15 — Jesus made a scourge and drove them out and overthrew the tables.

teens.question: Who told Jesus that the wedding had run out of wine?
teens.option_a: His mother Mary
teens.option_b: His disciple John
teens.option_c: The governor of the feast
teens.explanation: John 2:3 — "the mother of Jesus saith unto him, They have no wine."

teens.question: How many waterpots of stone were there, and how much did each hold?
teens.option_a: Six waterpots, holding two or three firkins each
teens.option_b: Four waterpots, holding one firkin each
teens.option_c: Ten waterpots, holding five firkins each
teens.option_d: Two waterpots, holding ten firkins each
teens.explanation: John 2:6 — "six waterpots of stone… containing two or three firkins apiece."

teens.question: What did the governor of the feast say about when good wine is usually served?
teens.option_a: At the beginning of the feast, not at the end
teens.option_b: At the end of the feast as a surprise
teens.option_c: Only to the most important guests
teens.option_d: Only after the meal is finished
teens.explanation: John 2:10 — "Every man at the beginning doth set forth good wine… thou hast kept the good wine until now."

teens.question: When Jesus said "Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up," what temple did He mean?
teens.option_a: His own body
teens.option_b: Herod's temple in Jerusalem
teens.option_c: A new heavenly temple
teens.explanation: John 2:21 — "he spake of the temple of his body."

teens.question: What was this miracle at Cana described as in the Gospel of John?
teens.option_a: The beginning of miracles (signs)
teens.option_b: The greatest miracle of all
teens.option_c: A secret miracle known only to the disciples
teens.explanation: John 2:11 — "This beginning of miracles did Jesus in Cana of Galilee."

adults.question: What exact words did Jesus speak to Mary when she mentioned the wine shortage?
adults.option_a: "Woman, what have I to do with thee? mine hour is not yet come"
adults.option_b: "Mother, it is not yet my time to act"
adults.option_c: "Why do you trouble me with this matter?"
adults.option_d: "Be patient, for the Father's will must be done"
adults.explanation: John 2:4 — "Woman, what have I to do with thee? mine hour is not yet come."

adults.question: What did Mary instruct the servants to do after Jesus' response?
adults.option_a: "Whatsoever he saith unto you, do it"
adults.option_b: "Bring more water from the well"
adults.option_c: "Wait for his command"
adults.option_d: "Fill the pots with new wine"
adults.explanation: John 2:5 — "His mother saith unto the servants, Whatsoever he saith unto you, do it."

adults.question: What items did Jesus use to drive out the merchants from the temple?
adults.option_a: A scourge of small cords
adults.option_b: A rod of iron
adults.option_c: A staff of wood
adults.option_d: His bare hands only
adults.explanation: John 2:15 — "when he had made a scourge of small cords, he drove them all out."

adults.question: What Old Testament passage did the disciples remember when Jesus cleansed the temple?
adults.option_a: "The zeal of thine house hath eaten me up" (Psalm 69:9)
adults.option_b: "My house shall be called a house of prayer" (Isaiah 56:7)
adults.option_c: "The Lord whom ye seek shall suddenly come to his temple" (Malachi 3:1)
adults.option_d: "Holiness becometh thine house" (Psalm 93:5)
adults.explanation: John 2:17 — "His disciples remembered that it was written, The zeal of thine house hath eaten me up."

adults.question: How long did the Jews say the temple had been under construction?
adults.option_a: Forty and six years
adults.option_b: Thirty years
adults.option_c: Twenty years
adults.option_d: Seventy years
adults.explanation: John 2:20 — "Forty and six years was this temple in building."

adults.question: Why did Jesus not commit Himself to those who believed during the Passover feast?
adults.option_a: Because He knew all men and what was in man
adults.option_b: Because they were Pharisees in disguise
adults.option_c: Because they only wanted earthly bread
adults.option_d: Because the time had not yet come
adults.explanation: John 2:24-25 — "Jesus did not commit himself unto them, because he knew all men."

adults.question: What theological significance does John attach to the water-to-wine miracle?
adults.option_a: It manifested His glory and His disciples believed on Him
adults.option_b: It proved He was greater than Moses
adults.option_c: It fulfilled the prophecy of the Messianic banquet
adults.option_d: It showed His authority over nature
adults.explanation: John 2:11 — "manifested forth his glory; and his disciples believed on him."

adults.question: After the wedding at Cana, where did Jesus go with His family and disciples?
adults.option_a: Capernaum, where they stayed not many days
adults.option_b: Jerusalem for the Passover
adults.option_c: Nazareth to His hometown
adults.option_d: Bethany beyond Jordan
adults.explanation: John 2:12 — "he went down to Capernaum… and they continued there not many days."

kids.question: Who came to talk to Jesus at night?
kids.explanation: John 3:1-2 — Nicodemus came to Jesus by night.

kids.question: What did Jesus say a person must be to see the kingdom of God?
kids.explanation: John 3:3 — "Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God."

kids.question: In John 3:16, what did God give because He loved the world?
kids.explanation: John 3:16 — "God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son."

teens.question: What group did Nicodemus belong to?
teens.option_a: The Pharisees and rulers of the Jews
teens.option_b: The Sadducees
teens.option_c: The Essenes
teens.explanation: John 3:1 — "a man of the Pharisees, named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews."

teens.question: What two things did Jesus say a man must be born of to enter the kingdom of God?
teens.option_a: Water and the Spirit
teens.option_b: Fire and the Spirit
teens.option_c: Blood and water
teens.explanation: John 3:5 — "Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God."

teens.question: What Old Testament event did Jesus compare to His being "lifted up"?
teens.option_a: Moses lifting up the serpent in the wilderness
teens.option_b: Elijah being taken up in a chariot of fire
teens.option_c: The ark being lifted above the flood waters
teens.option_d: Abraham offering Isaac on the mountain
teens.explanation: John 3:14 — "as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of man be lifted up."

teens.question: According to John 3:16, what happens to whoever believes in God's Son?
teens.option_a: They shall not perish but have everlasting life
teens.option_b: They shall be healed of all diseases
teens.option_c: They shall become rulers of the earth
teens.option_d: They shall never face any trials
teens.explanation: John 3:16 — "whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life."

teens.question: What did John the Baptist say must happen regarding himself and Jesus?
teens.option_a: "He must increase, but I must decrease"
teens.option_b: "We must serve together as equals"
teens.option_c: "I must prepare His throne"
teens.option_d: "He must teach and I must baptize"
teens.explanation: John 3:30 — "He must increase, but I must decrease."

adults.question: What title did Nicodemus use for Jesus, and what reason did he give?
adults.option_a: Rabbi — "no man can do these miracles that thou doest, except God be with him"
adults.option_b: Lord — "thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God"
adults.option_c: Prophet — "we have heard of thy fame in all the land"
adults.option_d: Master — "thy words are spirit and life"
adults.explanation: John 3:2 — "Rabbi, we know that thou art a teacher come from God: for no man can do these miracles… except God be with him."

adults.question: What rebuke did Jesus give Nicodemus regarding his understanding?
adults.option_a: "Art thou a master of Israel, and knowest not these things?"
adults.option_b: "You have studied the law but missed its meaning"
adults.option_c: "How can you teach others when you have not been taught?"
adults.option_d: "Even the common people understand what you cannot"
adults.explanation: John 3:10 — "Art thou a master of Israel, and knowest not these things?"

adults.question: In John 3:13, who does Jesus say has ascended to heaven?
adults.option_a: No man — only the Son of man which came down from heaven
adults.option_b: Elijah who was taken up in a whirlwind
adults.option_c: Moses who spoke with God on the mountain
adults.option_d: Enoch who walked with God
adults.explanation: John 3:13 — "no man hath ascended up to heaven, but he that came down from heaven, even the Son of man."

adults.question: According to John 3:17-18, why was the Son sent into the world?
adults.option_a: Not to condemn the world, but that the world through Him might be saved
adults.option_b: To judge between the righteous and the wicked
adults.option_c: To establish the kingdom of God on earth
adults.option_d: To destroy the works of the devil
adults.explanation: John 3:17 — "God sent not his Son into the world to condemn the world; but that the world through him might be saved."

adults.question: What does John 3:19 identify as the condemnation of mankind?
adults.option_a: Light came into the world, and men loved darkness rather than light
adults.option_b: Men rejected the prophets sent to them
adults.option_c: Men worshipped idols instead of the true God
adults.option_d: Men trusted in their own works
adults.explanation: John 3:19 — "this is the condemnation, that light is come into the world, and men loved darkness rather than light."

adults.question: What dispute arose between John the Baptist's disciples and the Jews?
adults.option_a: A question about purifying (ceremonial washing)
adults.option_b: A debate about the coming Messiah
adults.option_c: A disagreement about Sabbath observance
adults.option_d: A conflict about temple sacrifices
adults.explanation: John 3:25 — "there arose a question between some of John's disciples and the Jews about purifying."

adults.question: What analogy does Jesus use for the work of the Spirit in regeneration?
adults.option_a: The wind blows where it wishes — you hear the sound but cannot tell where it comes from or goes
adults.option_b: Water flowing from a spring into a river
adults.option_c: Fire consuming dry wood
adults.option_d: A seed growing secretly in the earth
adults.explanation: John 3:8 — "The wind bloweth where it listeth, and thou hearest the sound thereof, but canst not tell whence it cometh."

adults.question: What does John 3:36 say about the person who does not believe the Son?
adults.option_a: He shall not see life, and the wrath of God abideth on him
adults.option_b: He will be given another chance at judgment
adults.option_c: He will simply cease to exist
adults.option_d: He will be sent to a place of waiting
adults.explanation: John 3:36 — "he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on him."

kids.question: Where did Jesus meet a woman and ask her for a drink of water?
kids.explanation: John 4:6-7 — Jesus sat on Jacob's well in Samaria and asked the woman for a drink.

kids.question: What special kind of water did Jesus offer the woman?
kids.explanation: John 4:10 — Jesus said He would give her "living water."

kids.question: What happened to the nobleman's son after Jesus spoke a word?
kids.explanation: John 4:50-53 — Jesus said "Thy son liveth" and the boy was healed.

teens.question: Why was the Samaritan woman surprised that Jesus spoke to her?
teens.option_a: Jews had no dealings with Samaritans
teens.option_b: Women were not allowed to speak to strangers
teens.option_c: She was a known sinner in the town
teens.option_d: He was a rabbi and she was uneducated
teens.explanation: John 4:9 — "the Jews have no dealings with the Samaritans."

teens.question: How many husbands had the woman at the well had?
teens.option_a: Five, and the man she was with was not her husband
teens.option_b: Three, and she was a widow
teens.option_c: Seven, and she had been divorced by each
teens.option_d: Two, and she was looking for a third
teens.explanation: John 4:18 — "thou hast had five husbands; and he whom thou now hast is not thy husband."

teens.question: What did Jesus say about how true worshippers must worship God?
teens.option_a: In spirit and in truth
teens.option_b: In the temple at Jerusalem only
teens.option_c: With sacrifices and offerings
teens.explanation: John 4:23-24 — "true worshippers shall worship the Father in spirit and in truth."

teens.question: What did the Samaritan woman do after speaking with Jesus?
teens.option_a: She left her waterpot and told the men of the city about Jesus
teens.option_b: She brought her husband to meet Jesus
teens.option_c: She began to weep and ask for forgiveness
teens.option_d: She invited Jesus to her home for dinner
teens.explanation: John 4:28-29 — She left her waterpot and said "Come, see a man, which told me all things that ever I did."

teens.question: How long did the Samaritans ask Jesus to stay with them?
teens.option_a: Two days
teens.option_b: Seven days
teens.option_c: One day
teens.explanation: John 4:40 — "they besought him that he would tarry with them: and he abode there two days."

adults.question: Whose well was the Samaritan woman drawing from, according to the text?
adults.option_a: Jacob's well, near the parcel of ground Jacob gave to Joseph
adults.option_b: Abraham's well at Beer-sheba
adults.option_c: Moses' well in the wilderness
adults.option_d: Isaac's well at Gerar
adults.explanation: John 4:5-6 — "Jacob's well was there." The land was near what "Jacob gave to his son Joseph."

adults.question: What hour was it when Jesus sat on the well?
adults.option_a: About the sixth hour (noon)
adults.option_b: About the third hour (9 AM)
adults.option_c: About the ninth hour (3 PM)
adults.option_d: About the tenth hour (4 PM)
adults.explanation: John 4:6 — "it was about the sixth hour."

adults.question: What did Jesus describe as His "meat" (food)?
adults.option_a: To do the will of Him that sent Him and to finish His work
adults.option_b: The bread of life that comes from heaven
adults.option_c: The Word of God alone
adults.option_d: Prayer and fasting before the Father
adults.explanation: John 4:34 — "My meat is to do the will of him that sent me, and to finish his work."

adults.question: What agricultural metaphor did Jesus use about the harvest in Samaria?
adults.option_a: "Lift up your eyes and look on the fields; for they are white already to harvest"
adults.option_b: "The sower and the reaper shall rejoice together at the end"
adults.option_c: "The wheat and the tares grow together until the harvest"
adults.option_d: "First the blade, then the ear, then the full corn"
adults.explanation: John 4:35 — "Lift up your eyes, and look on the fields; for they are white already to harvest."

adults.question: What did the Samaritans ultimately confess about Jesus after His two-day stay?
adults.option_a: "This is indeed the Christ, the Saviour of the world"
adults.option_b: "This man is truly a great prophet"
adults.option_c: "He is the one Moses wrote about"
adults.option_d: "He is the Son of David who was promised"
adults.explanation: John 4:42 — "this is indeed the Christ, the Saviour of the world."

adults.question: Where was the nobleman from, and where was his sick son?
adults.option_a: The nobleman was in Cana; his son was in Capernaum
adults.option_b: The nobleman was in Jerusalem; his son was in Bethany
adults.option_c: The nobleman was in Nazareth; his son was in Cana
adults.option_d: The nobleman was in Bethsaida; his son was in Tiberias
adults.explanation: John 4:46-47 — Jesus was in Cana; the nobleman came to Him, and his son was sick in Capernaum.

adults.question: What did Jesus say to the nobleman that initially seemed like a rebuke?
adults.option_a: "Except ye see signs and wonders, ye will not believe"
adults.option_b: "O ye of little faith, why do you doubt?"
adults.option_c: "Your sins have caused this illness"
adults.option_d: "Go first and offer sacrifice in the temple"
adults.explanation: John 4:48 — "Except ye see signs and wonders, ye will not believe."

adults.question: What number sign/miracle in John's Gospel was the healing of the nobleman's son?
adults.option_a: The second miracle that Jesus did in Cana of Galilee
adults.option_b: The third sign performed in Judea
adults.option_c: The first miracle after leaving Jerusalem
adults.option_d: The fourth sign of His ministry
adults.explanation: John 4:54 — "This is again the second miracle that Jesus did, when he was come out of Judaea into Galilee."

kids.question: How long had the man at the pool been sick?
kids.explanation: John 5:5 — "a certain man was there, which had an infirmity thirty and eight years."

kids.question: What did Jesus tell the sick man to do?
kids.option_a: "Rise, take up thy bed, and walk"
kids.explanation: John 5:8 — "Jesus saith unto him, Rise, take up thy bed, and walk."

kids.question: What day of the week did Jesus heal the man at the pool?
kids.option_b: The first day of the week
kids.explanation: John 5:9 — "and on the same day was the sabbath."

teens.question: What was the name of the pool where Jesus healed the man?
teens.option_a: Bethesda
teens.option_b: Siloam
teens.option_c: Bethany
teens.explanation: John 5:2 — "a pool, which is called in the Hebrew tongue Bethesda."

teens.question: Why were the Jewish leaders upset with the healed man?
teens.option_a: He was carrying his bed on the Sabbath
teens.option_b: He was shouting in the temple
teens.option_c: He did not thank the priests first
teens.option_d: He told everyone about the healing
teens.explanation: John 5:10 — "The Jews therefore said unto him that was cured, It is the sabbath day: it is not lawful for thee to carry thy bed."

teens.question: What did Jesus warn the healed man about when He found him in the temple?
teens.option_a: "Sin no more, lest a worse thing come unto thee"
teens.option_b: "Go and show thyself to the priest"
teens.option_c: "Tell no man about this healing"
teens.explanation: John 5:14 — "sin no more, lest a worse thing come unto thee."

teens.question: Why did the Jews seek to kill Jesus after this healing?
teens.option_a: He healed on the Sabbath and said God was His Father, making Himself equal with God
teens.option_b: He claimed to be greater than Moses
teens.option_c: He refused to obey the temple authorities
teens.option_d: He told the man not to follow Jewish law
teens.explanation: John 5:18 — "he had broken the sabbath, and said also that God was his Father, making himself equal with God."

teens.question: According to Jesus, who will hear the voice of the Son of God and live?
teens.option_a: The dead — and the hour is coming and now is
teens.option_b: Only the righteous who keep the law
teens.option_c: Only those who are baptized
teens.option_d: Only the priests in the temple
teens.explanation: John 5:25 — "The hour is coming, and now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God: and they that hear shall live."

adults.question: How many porches surrounded the pool of Bethesda?
adults.option_a: Five
adults.option_b: Three
adults.option_c: Four
adults.option_d: Seven
adults.explanation: John 5:2 — "a pool, which is called in the Hebrew tongue Bethesda, having five porches."

adults.question: What did Jesus claim about His relationship to the Father's ongoing work?
adults.option_a: "My Father worketh hitherto, and I work"
adults.option_b: "I do only what the prophets commanded"
adults.option_c: "The Father rests, but I continue His plan"
adults.option_d: "I work by the power of the Spirit alone"
adults.explanation: John 5:17 — "My Father worketh hitherto, and I work."

adults.question: According to John 5:22, what has the Father committed entirely to the Son?
adults.option_a: All judgment
adults.option_b: All creation
adults.option_c: All prophecy
adults.option_d: All healing
adults.explanation: John 5:22 — "the Father judgeth no man, but hath committed all judgment unto the Son."

adults.question: What four witnesses to Jesus does He enumerate in chapter 5?
adults.option_a: John the Baptist, His works, the Father, and the Scriptures
adults.option_b: Moses, Elijah, the prophets, and the angels
adults.option_c: Peter, James, John, and Andrew
adults.option_d: The miracles, the temple, the law, and the sacrifices
adults.explanation: John 5:33-39 — John's testimony (v33), Jesus' works (v36), the Father (v37), and the Scriptures (v39).

adults.question: What did Jesus say the Jews searched the Scriptures for, thinking they had it in them?
adults.option_a: Eternal life — and "they are they which testify of me"
adults.option_b: The secrets of God's kingdom
adults.option_c: The exact date of the Messiah's coming
adults.option_d: Wisdom and understanding of the law
adults.explanation: John 5:39 — "Search the scriptures; for in them ye think ye have eternal life: and they are they which testify of me."

adults.question: Whom did Jesus say would accuse the Jews to the Father?
adults.option_a: Moses, in whom they trusted
adults.option_b: Elijah the prophet
adults.option_c: John the Baptist
adults.option_d: The angels of God
adults.explanation: John 5:45 — "there is one that accuseth you, even Moses, in whom ye trust."

adults.question: What two types of resurrection does Jesus describe in John 5:29?
adults.option_a: The resurrection of life and the resurrection of damnation
adults.option_b: The resurrection of the body and the resurrection of the spirit
adults.option_c: The first resurrection and the final resurrection
adults.option_d: The resurrection of the righteous and the sleep of the wicked
adults.explanation: John 5:29 — "they that have done good, unto the resurrection of life; and they that have done evil, unto the resurrection of damnation."

adults.question: What reason did Jesus give for why the Jews could not believe in Him?
adults.option_a: They received honour from one another and did not seek the honour that comes from God only
adults.option_b: They were blinded by Satan's deceptions
adults.option_c: They had never read the Scriptures properly
adults.option_d: They were afraid of losing their positions of power
adults.explanation: John 5:44 — "How can ye believe, which receive honour one of another, and seek not the honour that cometh from God only?"

kids.question: How many people did Jesus feed with a boy's lunch?
kids.explanation: John 6:10 — "the men sat down, in number about five thousand."

kids.question: What food did the boy have that Jesus used to feed the crowd?
kids.option_a: Five barley loaves and two small fishes
kids.option_b: Seven loaves and a few fishes
kids.explanation: John 6:9 — "five barley loaves, and two small fishes."

kids.question: What amazing thing did Jesus do on the sea that night?
kids.explanation: John 6:19 — "they see Jesus walking on the sea."

teens.question: How many baskets of leftovers were collected after the feeding of the five thousand?
teens.option_a: Twelve baskets
teens.option_b: Seven baskets
teens.option_c: Five baskets
teens.explanation: John 6:13 — "filled twelve baskets with the fragments."

teens.question: What did Jesus say to the disciples when they were afraid of Him on the water?
teens.option_a: "It is I; be not afraid"
teens.option_b: "Peace, be still"
teens.option_c: "O ye of little faith"
teens.explanation: John 6:20 — "It is I; be not afraid."

teens.question: What title did Jesus give Himself in this chapter about bread?
teens.option_a: "I am the bread of life"
teens.option_b: "I am the door of the sheep"
teens.option_c: "I am the good shepherd"
teens.explanation: John 6:35 — "I am the bread of life."

teens.question: Why did many of Jesus' disciples stop following Him after His teaching in this chapter?
teens.option_a: They said "This is an hard saying; who can hear it?"
teens.option_b: They were afraid of the Pharisees
teens.option_c: They wanted to make Him king by force
teens.explanation: John 6:60,66 — "Many therefore of his disciples… said, This is an hard saying; who can hear it?" and "many of his disciples went back."

teens.question: What did Peter say when Jesus asked if the twelve would also leave?
teens.option_a: "Lord, to whom shall we go? thou hast the words of eternal life"
teens.option_b: "We will never leave you, Master"
teens.option_c: "Where else can we find such miracles?"
teens.option_d: "We have given up everything for you"
teens.explanation: John 6:68 — "Lord, to whom shall we go? thou hast the words of eternal life."

adults.question: Which disciple did Jesus test by asking where they could buy bread for the multitude?
adults.option_a: Philip
adults.option_b: Andrew
adults.option_c: Peter
adults.option_d: John
adults.explanation: John 6:5-6 — Jesus asked Philip, "Whence shall we buy bread, that these may eat? And this he said to prove him."

adults.question: What did the people try to do after witnessing the feeding miracle?
adults.option_a: Take Jesus by force to make Him a king
adults.option_b: Bring more sick people for healing
adults.option_c: Follow Him across the sea
adults.option_d: Declare Him the new high priest
adults.explanation: John 6:15 — "they would come and take him by force, to make him a king."

adults.question: What does Jesus say is the will of the Father regarding those given to Him?
adults.option_a: That He should lose nothing, but raise it up again at the last day
adults.option_b: That they should keep all the commandments
adults.option_c: That they should preach to every nation
adults.option_d: That they should fast and pray continually
adults.explanation: John 6:39 — "of all which he hath given me I should lose nothing, but should raise it up again at the last day."

adults.question: What Old Testament comparison did the crowd make, asking Jesus to match it?
adults.option_a: Their fathers ate manna in the desert — they wanted similar bread from heaven
adults.option_b: Moses parted the Red Sea — they wanted Him to part the Jordan
adults.option_c: Elijah called fire from heaven — they wanted a similar sign
adults.option_d: Joshua stopped the sun — they wanted power over nature
adults.explanation: John 6:31 — "Our fathers did eat manna in the desert; as it is written, He gave them bread from heaven to eat."

adults.question: What does Jesus teach about eating His flesh and drinking His blood in John 6:53-54?
adults.option_a: Unless you eat His flesh and drink His blood, you have no life — those who do have eternal life
adults.option_b: It is a commandment to observe the Passover meal in His honor
adults.option_c: It is symbolic language meaning to follow His teachings
adults.option_d: It means to fast in remembrance of His sacrifice
adults.explanation: John 6:53-54 — "Except ye eat the flesh of the Son of man, and drink his blood, ye have no life in you."

adults.question: What did Jesus say about those who come to Him and their spiritual state?
adults.option_a: "No man can come to me, except the Father which hath sent me draw him"
adults.option_b: "All who seek shall find, for the door is always open"
adults.option_c: "Those who keep the law are already near to me"
adults.option_d: "Whoever hears the prophets will come to me naturally"
adults.explanation: John 6:44 — "No man can come to me, except the Father which hath sent me draw him."

adults.question: Which disciple did Jesus identify as a devil (betrayer) at the end of chapter 6?
adults.option_a: Judas Iscariot, though He did not name him directly — John says Jesus spoke of him
adults.option_b: Peter, for his lack of faith
adults.option_c: Thomas, for his doubts
adults.option_d: Bartholomew, for his unbelief
adults.explanation: John 6:70-71 — "one of you is a devil… He spake of Judas Iscariot."

adults.question: How does Jesus contrast the manna of Moses with the true bread from heaven?
adults.option_a: The fathers ate manna and are dead, but whoever eats the living bread shall live forever
adults.option_b: The manna sustained only the body, but the true bread heals all diseases
adults.option_c: The manna was for Israel only, but the bread of life is for the Gentiles
adults.option_d: The manna was temporary food, but the new bread is the written Word of God
adults.explanation: John 6:49-51 — "Your fathers did eat manna in the wilderness, and are dead… if any man eat of this bread, he shall live for ever."

kids.question: What Jewish feast was happening in this chapter?
kids.explanation: John 7:2 — "the Jews' feast of tabernacles was at hand."

kids.question: What did Jesus offer to anyone who was thirsty?
kids.explanation: John 7:37 — "If any man thirst, let him come unto me, and drink."

kids.question: Did the people all agree about who Jesus was?
kids.option_a: No, there was a division among the people about Him
kids.option_b: Yes, everyone believed He was the Christ
kids.option_c: Yes, everyone thought He was just a teacher
kids.option_d: No one had an opinion about Him
kids.explanation: John 7:43 — "So there was a division among the people because of him."

teens.question: Why did Jesus' own brothers tell Him to go to Judaea?
teens.option_a: They said He should show His works publicly if He wanted followers — they did not believe in Him
teens.option_b: They wanted Him to be safe with family there
teens.option_c: They wanted Him to visit the temple for prayer
teens.option_d: They believed He was the Messiah and should declare it
teens.explanation: John 7:3-5 — "His brethren… said unto him… shew thyself to the world. For neither did his brethren believe in him."

teens.question: How did Jesus go to the feast?
teens.option_a: Secretly, not openly
teens.option_b: With a large crowd of followers
teens.option_c: With His brothers and mother
teens.explanation: John 7:10 — "then went he also up unto the feast, not openly, but as it were in secret."

teens.question: What did the officers sent to arrest Jesus say when they returned empty-handed?
teens.option_a: "Never man spake like this man"
teens.option_b: "He escaped before we could reach Him"
teens.option_c: "The crowd protected Him from us"
teens.option_d: "We could not find Him in the temple"
teens.explanation: John 7:46 — "The officers answered, Never man spake like this man."

teens.question: Who spoke up in Jesus' defense among the Pharisees?
teens.option_a: Nicodemus
teens.option_b: Joseph of Arimathaea
teens.option_c: Gamaliel
teens.explanation: John 7:50-51 — "Nicodemus saith unto them… Doth our law judge any man, before it hear him?"

teens.question: What was the crowd's debate about Jesus' origin that caused confusion?
teens.option_a: Some said Christ comes from Bethlehem, not Galilee, unaware Jesus was born in Bethlehem
teens.option_b: They argued whether He was from Nazareth or Jerusalem
teens.option_c: They debated if He was from the tribe of Judah or Levi
teens.option_d: They disagreed about whether He was a prophet or a priest
teens.explanation: John 7:41-42 — "Shall Christ come out of Galilee? Hath not the scripture said, That Christ cometh of the seed of David, and out of the town of Bethlehem?"

adults.question: What reason did Jesus give for the world's hatred toward Him?
adults.option_a: "The world… hateth me, because I testify of it, that the works thereof are evil"
adults.option_b: "The world hates me because I am not of this world"
adults.option_c: "They hate me because I come from Galilee"
adults.option_d: "The rulers stir up hatred because they fear losing power"
adults.explanation: John 7:7 — "The world… hateth me, because I testify of it, that the works thereof are evil."

adults.question: What test did Jesus give to know whether His doctrine was from God?
adults.option_a: "If any man will do his will, he shall know of the doctrine"
adults.option_b: "Search the scriptures and compare them to my words"
adults.option_c: "Ask the Father in prayer and He will confirm it"
adults.option_d: "Watch for signs and wonders that follow my teaching"
adults.explanation: John 7:17 — "If any man will do his will, he shall know of the doctrine, whether it be of God."

adults.question: What argument about circumcision and the Sabbath did Jesus use?
adults.option_a: If circumcision is done on the Sabbath to keep Moses' law, why are they angry He healed a whole man on the Sabbath?
adults.option_b: Moses never intended the Sabbath to prevent mercy
adults.option_c: Abraham circumcised on every day of the week
adults.option_d: The prophets regularly healed on the Sabbath
adults.explanation: John 7:23 — "If a man on the sabbath day receive circumcision… are ye angry at me, because I have made a man every whit whole on the sabbath day?"

adults.question: What did Jesus promise would flow out of those who believe in Him?
adults.option_a: Rivers of living water — which John says refers to the Holy Spirit
adults.option_b: Streams of healing power
adults.option_c: Words of prophecy and wisdom
adults.option_d: Songs of praise and thanksgiving
adults.explanation: John 7:38-39 — "out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water. But this spake he of the Spirit."

adults.question: What was Jesus' response about His time or "hour"?
adults.option_a: "My time is not yet come: but your time is alway ready"
adults.option_b: "The hour approaches when all will be revealed"
adults.option_c: "My time is in the Father's hands alone"
adults.option_d: "When the feast is over, then my hour will come"
adults.explanation: John 7:6 — "My time is not yet come: but your time is alway ready."

adults.question: According to John 7:15, what amazed the Jews about Jesus' teaching?
adults.option_a: He knew letters (Scripture) having never formally studied
adults.option_b: He spoke with more authority than the scribes
adults.option_c: He could quote every book of Moses from memory
adults.option_d: He taught in multiple languages
adults.explanation: John 7:15 — "the Jews marvelled, saying, How knoweth this man letters, having never learned?"

adults.question: What did Jesus cry out on the "last day, that great day of the feast"?
adults.option_a: "If any man thirst, let him come unto me, and drink"
adults.option_b: "I am the light of the world"
adults.option_c: "The hour is come for the Son of man to be glorified"
adults.option_d: "Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand"
adults.explanation: John 7:37 — "In the last day, that great day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried, saying, If any man thirst, let him come unto me, and drink."

adults.question: What principle did Nicodemus cite in defense of Jesus before the Pharisees?
adults.option_a: Their law does not judge a man before hearing him and knowing what he does
adults.option_b: The prophets foretold that the Messiah would come from Galilee
adults.option_c: A man's works testify to his character
adults.option_d: The Sanhedrin must have a unanimous vote to condemn
adults.explanation: John 7:51 — "Doth our law judge any man, before it hear him, and know what he doeth?"

kids.question: A woman was brought to Jesus who had done something wrong. What did Jesus say to those who wanted to punish her?
kids.option_a: "He that is without sin among you, let him first cast a stone at her"
kids.option_b: "Take her to the priests for judgment"
kids.explanation: John 8:7 — "He that is without sin among you, let him first cast a stone at her."

kids.question: What did Jesus call Himself about light?
kids.option_a: "I am the light of the world"
kids.explanation: John 8:12 — "I am the light of the world."

kids.question: What did all the accusers do after Jesus spoke to them?
kids.option_a: They left one by one, beginning with the oldest
kids.option_b: They threw stones at Jesus instead
kids.option_c: They asked Jesus to judge the woman
kids.explanation: John 8:9 — "went out one by one, beginning at the eldest."

teens.question: After Jesus spoke to the woman's accusers, who left first?
teens.option_a: The eldest went out first, then the others one by one
teens.option_b: The youngest left first out of shame
teens.option_c: The scribes left, but the Pharisees stayed
teens.option_d: They all left at the same time
teens.explanation: John 8:9 — "they which heard it, being convicted by their own conscience, went out one by one, beginning at the eldest."

teens.question: What did Jesus tell the woman after her accusers left?
teens.option_a: "Neither do I condemn thee: go, and sin no more"
teens.option_b: "Your sins are forgiven, go in peace"
teens.option_c: "Go and show yourself to the priest"
teens.explanation: John 8:11 — "Neither do I condemn thee: go, and sin no more."

teens.question: What did Jesus promise to those who continue in His word?
teens.option_a: "Ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free"
teens.option_b: "You shall have treasure in heaven"
teens.option_c: "You shall never be sick again"
teens.explanation: John 8:31-32 — "If ye continue in my word… ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free."

teens.question: What claim about Abraham made the Jews want to stone Jesus?
teens.option_a: "Before Abraham was, I am"
teens.option_b: "I am greater than Abraham"
teens.option_c: "Abraham was my servant"
teens.explanation: John 8:58-59 — "Before Abraham was, I am. Then took they up stones to cast at him."

teens.question: What did Jesus say about the devil?
teens.option_a: "He was a murderer from the beginning, and abode not in the truth"
teens.option_b: "He is a fallen angel of the highest order"
teens.option_c: "He was once a servant of God who turned away"
teens.option_d: "He rules the kingdoms of this world temporarily"
teens.explanation: John 8:44 — "He was a murderer from the beginning, and abode not in the truth."

adults.question: What law did the scribes and Pharisees cite regarding the woman caught in adultery?
adults.option_a: Moses in the law commanded that such should be stoned
adults.option_b: Moses said adulterers should be put out of the camp
adults.option_c: The law required two witnesses for a death sentence
adults.option_d: Moses commanded a certificate of divorce
adults.explanation: John 8:5 — "Moses in the law commanded us, that such should be stoned."

adults.question: When Jesus declared "I am the light of the world," what did He promise to those who follow Him?
adults.option_a: They shall not walk in darkness but shall have the light of life
adults.option_b: They shall see the glory of God in the temple
adults.option_c: They shall perform mighty works in His name
adults.option_d: They shall be called children of light
adults.explanation: John 8:12 — "he that followeth me shall not walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life."

adults.question: What did Jesus say about His judgment?
adults.option_a: "I judge no man. And yet if I judge, my judgment is true: for I am not alone, but I and the Father that sent me"
adults.option_b: "My judgment is righteous because I know the hearts of all men"
adults.option_c: "I leave all judgment to the Father until the last day"
adults.option_d: "I judge by the law of Moses and the prophets"
adults.explanation: John 8:15-16 — "I judge no man. And yet if I judge, my judgment is true: for I am not alone."

adults.question: What did Jesus say would happen if the Jews did not believe that "I am he"?
adults.option_a: "Ye shall die in your sins"
adults.option_b: "Ye shall be cast out of the synagogue"
adults.option_c: "Ye shall wander without hope"
adults.option_d: "Ye shall face the judgment of Moses"
adults.explanation: John 8:24 — "if ye believe not that I am he, ye shall die in your sins."

adults.question: Whom did Jesus identify as the father of the unbelieving Jews in this chapter?
adults.option_a: The devil — "Ye are of your father the devil, and the lusts of your father ye will do"
adults.option_b: Abraham in name only, not in truth
adults.option_c: Moses, whose law they misunderstand
adults.option_d: The Pharisees who taught them
adults.explanation: John 8:44 — "Ye are of your father the devil."

adults.question: What did Jesus claim about His relationship to Abraham?
adults.option_a: "Your father Abraham rejoiced to see my day: and he saw it, and was glad"
adults.option_b: "Abraham worshipped me in the tent at Mamre"
adults.option_c: "Abraham received the covenant from my hands"
adults.option_d: "I appeared to Abraham in the burning bush"
adults.explanation: John 8:56 — "Your father Abraham rejoiced to see my day: and he saw it, and was glad."

adults.question: What is the theological significance of Jesus' statement "Before Abraham was, I am"?
adults.option_a: It echoes God's self-revelation as "I AM" in Exodus 3:14, claiming divine pre-existence and deity
adults.option_b: It simply means Jesus was older than Abraham in heavenly years
adults.option_c: It refers to Jesus' foreknowledge of Abraham's life
adults.option_d: It is a claim to be a reincarnation of Abraham
adults.explanation: John 8:58 — "Before Abraham was, I am" parallels the divine name revealed to Moses (Exodus 3:14).

adults.question: According to Jesus in John 8:34-36, what is the condition and remedy of those who commit sin?
adults.option_a: Whoever commits sin is the servant of sin; but if the Son makes you free, you are free indeed
adults.option_b: Sin leads to death, but the law can save
adults.option_c: All men sin but the righteous earn their freedom
adults.option_d: Sin is a disease that can be cured by repentance alone
adults.explanation: John 8:34,36 — "Whosoever committeth sin is the servant of sin… If the Son therefore shall make you free, ye shall be free indeed."

kids.question: What was wrong with the man Jesus healed in this chapter?
kids.explanation: John 9:1 — "a man which was blind from his birth."

kids.question: What did Jesus put on the blind man's eyes?
kids.option_a: Clay made from spit and dirt
kids.explanation: John 9:6 — "he spat on the ground, and made clay of the spittle, and he anointed the eyes of the blind man with the clay."

kids.question: Where did Jesus tell the blind man to go and wash?
kids.explanation: John 9:7 — "Go, wash in the pool of Siloam."

teens.question: Why did the disciples think the man was born blind?
teens.option_a: They asked if it was because of his sin or his parents' sin
teens.option_b: They thought he had been cursed by a priest
teens.option_c: They believed he had offended God in a past life
teens.option_d: They thought his mother had been near an unclean animal
teens.explanation: John 9:2 — "who did sin, this man, or his parents, that he was born blind?"

teens.question: What did Jesus say was the real reason the man was born blind?
teens.option_a: "That the works of God should be made manifest in him"
teens.option_b: "Because of the sins of his ancestors"
teens.option_c: "To teach his parents humility"
teens.explanation: John 9:3 — "Neither hath this man sinned, nor his parents: but that the works of God should be made manifest in him."

teens.question: What did the healed man say to the Pharisees about his experience?
teens.option_a: "One thing I know, that, whereas I was blind, now I see"
teens.option_b: "Jesus is the greatest prophet who ever lived"
teens.option_c: "I have never seen such a miracle before"
teens.option_d: "My eyes were opened by the power of God alone"
teens.explanation: John 9:25 — "One thing I know, that, whereas I was blind, now I see."

teens.question: What happened to the healed man when he confessed Jesus?
teens.option_a: The Pharisees cast him out of the synagogue
teens.option_b: The Pharisees praised him for his faith
teens.option_c: He was arrested and put in prison
teens.option_d: He was sent to the high priest for questioning
teens.explanation: John 9:34 — "they cast him out."

teens.question: When Jesus found the man after he was cast out, what did the man do?
teens.option_a: He worshipped Jesus after learning He was the Son of God
teens.option_b: He asked Jesus to heal others too
teens.option_c: He went and told the whole city
teens.option_d: He asked to become one of Jesus' disciples
teens.explanation: John 9:38 — "Lord, I believe. And he worshipped him."

adults.question: What did Jesus say about working while it is day?
adults.option_a: "I must work the works of him that sent me, while it is day: the night cometh, when no man can work"
adults.option_b: "Work while the harvest is plentiful and the laborers are few"
adults.option_c: "Do the Father's will before the Sabbath comes"
adults.option_d: "Make haste, for my hour is drawing near"
adults.explanation: John 9:4 — "I must work the works of him that sent me, while it is day: the night cometh, when no man can work."

adults.question: What meaning does John give for the name "Siloam"?
adults.option_a: Sent
adults.option_b: Healed
adults.option_c: Washed
adults.option_d: Chosen
adults.explanation: John 9:7 — "the pool of Siloam, (which is by interpretation, Sent)."

adults.question: Why were the Pharisees divided about Jesus after this healing?
adults.option_a: Some said He was not from God because He broke the Sabbath; others asked how a sinner could do such miracles
adults.option_b: Some wanted to follow Him; others wanted to arrest Him
adults.option_c: Some said He was Moses; others said He was Elijah
adults.option_d: Some praised God; others blamed the devil
adults.explanation: John 9:16 — "some of the Pharisees said, This man is not of God, because he keepeth not the sabbath day. Others said, How can a man that is a sinner do such miracles?"

adults.question: Why did the healed man's parents refuse to explain how he could now see?
adults.option_a: They feared the Jews, who had agreed to put out of the synagogue anyone who confessed Christ
adults.option_b: They did not believe their son had truly been blind
adults.option_c: They were ashamed of their son's association with Jesus
adults.option_d: They had been told by the priests not to speak
adults.explanation: John 9:22 — "because they feared the Jews: for the Jews had agreed already, that if any man did confess that he was Christ, he should be put out of the synagogue."

adults.question: What ironic statement did the healed man make to the Pharisees about Jesus' origin?
adults.option_a: "Herein is a marvellous thing, that ye know not from whence he is, and yet he hath opened mine eyes"
adults.option_b: "You claim to know the law but cannot see the truth"
adults.option_c: "If He were not from God, how could He do this?"
adults.option_d: "Even a blind man can see that God is with Him"
adults.explanation: John 9:30 — "Herein is a marvellous thing, that ye know not from whence he is, and yet he hath opened mine eyes."

adults.question: What statement does Jesus make about spiritual blindness at the end of chapter 9?
adults.option_a: "If ye were blind, ye should have no sin: but now ye say, We see; therefore your sin remaineth"
adults.option_b: "Those who are blind shall see, and those who see shall stumble"
adults.option_c: "The blind are blessed for they walk by faith"
adults.option_d: "Your blindness is self-imposed and cannot be healed"
adults.explanation: John 9:41 — "If ye were blind, ye should have no sin: but now ye say, We see; therefore your sin remaineth."

adults.question: What did Jesus declare about Himself at the beginning of this episode?
adults.option_a: "As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world"
adults.option_b: "I have come to give sight to all who believe"
adults.option_c: "The Father has sent me to heal the blind"
adults.option_d: "I am the door through which the blind shall see"
adults.explanation: John 9:5 — "As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world."

adults.question: What theological principle did the Pharisees claim about Jesus that the healed man challenged?
adults.option_a: They said "we know that this man is a sinner" — the healed man replied that God does not hear sinners but hears those who worship Him and do His will
adults.option_b: They said He was possessed by a demon — the man said demons cannot open blind eyes
adults.option_c: They said He was from Nazareth — the man said the Messiah's birthplace does not matter
adults.option_d: They said He had no authority — the man said His works proved His authority
adults.explanation: John 9:24,31 — "We know that this man is a sinner… Now we know that God heareth not sinners: but if any man be a worshipper of God, and doeth his will, him he heareth."

kids.question: What did Jesus call Himself in this chapter about a shepherd?
kids.explanation: John 10:11 — "I am the good shepherd."

kids.question: What does the good shepherd do for his sheep?
kids.option_a: He gives his life for the sheep
kids.option_c: He builds them a big barn
kids.explanation: John 10:11 — "the good shepherd giveth his life for the sheep."

kids.question: What did Jesus say about His sheep knowing Him?
kids.option_a: His sheep hear His voice and follow Him
kids.option_b: His sheep wear a special mark
kids.option_c: His sheep are the strongest in the flock
kids.option_d: His sheep stay in one place forever
kids.explanation: John 10:27 — "My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me."

teens.question: What two "I am" statements does Jesus make in this chapter?
teens.option_a: "I am the door" and "I am the good shepherd"
teens.option_b: "I am the way" and "I am the truth"
teens.option_c: "I am the bread" and "I am the vine"
teens.option_d: "I am the resurrection" and "I am the life"
teens.explanation: John 10:7,11 — "I am the door of the sheep" and "I am the good shepherd."

teens.question: What is the difference between the good shepherd and a hireling (hired worker)?
teens.option_a: The hireling flees when the wolf comes; the shepherd lays down his life
teens.option_b: The hireling feeds the sheep poorly; the shepherd gives them the best
teens.option_c: The hireling ignores sick sheep; the shepherd heals them
teens.option_d: The hireling keeps the sheep inside; the shepherd lets them roam free
teens.explanation: John 10:12-13 — "he that is an hireling… seeth the wolf coming, and leaveth the sheep, and fleeth."

teens.question: What did Jesus say about having "other sheep"?
teens.option_a: He has other sheep not of this fold — He must bring them also, and there shall be one fold and one shepherd
teens.option_b: He has sheep in every nation who already believe in Him
teens.option_c: He will create new sheep in the age to come
teens.option_d: He has sheep that even the disciples do not know about
teens.explanation: John 10:16 — "other sheep I have, which are not of this fold: them also I must bring… and there shall be one fold, and one shepherd."

teens.question: What bold claim did Jesus make that caused the Jews to take up stones?
teens.option_a: "I and my Father are one"
teens.option_b: "I am greater than Moses"
teens.option_c: "I existed before the world was made"
teens.option_d: "I will destroy the temple and rebuild it"
teens.explanation: John 10:30-31 — "I and my Father are one. Then the Jews took up stones again to stone him."

teens.question: What promise does Jesus give about eternal security in John 10:28-29?
teens.option_a: No one shall pluck His sheep out of His hand or the Father's hand
teens.option_b: His sheep will never face any danger or trial
teens.option_c: His sheep will live forever on earth
teens.option_d: His sheep will always be prosperous and healthy
teens.explanation: John 10:28-29 — "neither shall any man pluck them out of my hand… no man is able to pluck them out of my Father's hand."

adults.question: What does Jesus say the thief comes to do in contrast to His own purpose?
adults.option_a: The thief comes to steal, kill, and destroy; Jesus came that they might have life more abundantly
adults.option_b: The thief scatters the sheep; Jesus gathers them
adults.option_c: The thief breaks down the walls; Jesus builds them up
adults.option_d: The thief deceives with lies; Jesus teaches truth
adults.explanation: John 10:10 — "The thief cometh not, but for to steal, and to kill, and to destroy: I am come that they might have life, and that they might have it more abundantly."

adults.question: What Scripture did Jesus quote from the Psalms to defend His claim of deity?
adults.option_a: "I said, Ye are gods" from Psalm 82:6 — arguing that if Scripture called human judges gods, He is justified in calling Himself the Son of God
adults.option_b: "The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand" from Psalm 110
adults.option_c: "Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchizedek" from Psalm 110
adults.option_d: "The stone which the builders refused is become the head stone" from Psalm 118
adults.explanation: John 10:34-36 — "Is it not written in your law, I said, Ye are gods?" (Psalm 82:6).

adults.question: What does Jesus say about His authority to lay down His life?
adults.option_a: "No man taketh it from me, but I lay it down of myself. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again"
adults.option_b: "The Father will take my life when the appointed time comes"
adults.option_c: "I surrender my life as a ransom for many"
adults.option_d: "My life is in the Father's hands, not my own"
adults.explanation: John 10:18 — "No man taketh it from me, but I lay it down of myself."

adults.question: During which feast did the Jews surround Jesus in Solomon's porch and demand He plainly say if He was the Christ?
adults.option_a: The Feast of Dedication (Hanukkah)
adults.option_b: The Feast of Tabernacles
adults.option_c: The Passover
adults.option_d: The Feast of Weeks (Pentecost)
adults.explanation: John 10:22-24 — "it was at Jerusalem the feast of the dedication, and it was winter."

adults.question: What evidence did Jesus point to when asked if He was the Christ?
adults.option_a: The works He did in His Father's name bear witness of Him
adults.option_b: The testimony of John the Baptist who declared Him Messiah
adults.option_c: The fulfillment of Old Testament prophecies in His life
adults.option_d: The voice of God that spoke at His baptism
adults.explanation: John 10:25 — "the works that I do in my Father's name, they bear witness of me."

adults.question: Why did Jesus say the unbelieving Jews could not believe?
adults.option_a: "Ye believe not, because ye are not of my sheep"
adults.option_b: "Ye are blinded by the traditions of men"
adults.option_c: "Ye have not been chosen by the Father"
adults.option_d: "Ye love darkness rather than light"
adults.explanation: John 10:26 — "ye believe not, because ye are not of my sheep."

adults.question: Where did Jesus go after the Jews tried to stone Him at the Feast of Dedication?
adults.option_a: Beyond Jordan, to the place where John first baptized
adults.option_b: To Galilee to avoid the Jewish leaders
adults.option_c: To Bethany near Jerusalem
adults.option_d: To the wilderness of Judaea
adults.explanation: John 10:40 — "went away again beyond Jordan into the place where John at first baptized."

adults.question: What did many people say about Jesus when He was beyond Jordan?
adults.option_a: "John did no miracle: but all things that John spake of this man were true" — and many believed
adults.option_b: "This is truly the prophet that Moses foretold"
adults.option_c: "He is the king of Israel who was promised"
adults.option_d: "His miracles prove He is from God"
adults.explanation: John 10:41-42 — "John did no miracle: but all things that John spake of this man were true. And many believed on him there."

kids.question: Who did Jesus raise from the dead in this chapter?
kids.explanation: John 11:43-44 — Jesus called Lazarus out of the grave.

kids.question: What is the shortest verse in the Bible, found in this chapter?
kids.explanation: John 11:35 — "Jesus wept."

kids.question: How many days had Lazarus been in the grave before Jesus arrived?
kids.explanation: John 11:17 — "he had lain in the grave four days already."

teens.question: Who were the two sisters of Lazarus?
teens.option_a: Martha and Mary
teens.option_b: Elizabeth and Anna
teens.option_c: Salome and Joanna
teens.explanation: John 11:1 — "Lazarus, of Bethany, the town of Mary and her sister Martha."

teens.question: What great "I am" statement did Jesus make to Martha?
teens.option_a: "I am the resurrection, and the life"
teens.option_b: "I am the way, the truth, and the life"
teens.option_c: "I am the bread of life"
teens.explanation: John 11:25 — "I am the resurrection, and the life."

teens.question: What did Martha say she believed about Lazarus before Jesus raised him?
teens.option_a: He would rise again in the resurrection at the last day
teens.option_b: He was in heaven with Abraham
teens.option_c: He would sleep until the Messiah came
teens.option_d: He would be reincarnated in a new body
teens.explanation: John 11:24 — "I know that he shall rise again in the resurrection at the last day."

teens.question: What did Jesus say to Lazarus at the tomb?
teens.option_a: "Lazarus, come forth"
teens.option_b: "Arise and live again"
teens.option_c: "Be healed in the name of God"
teens.explanation: John 11:43 — "he cried with a loud voice, Lazarus, come forth."

teens.question: What did the high priest Caiaphas unintentionally prophesy?
teens.option_a: That Jesus should die for the nation, so the whole nation would not perish
teens.option_b: That Jesus would destroy the temple and rebuild it
teens.option_c: That Jesus would become the king of Israel
teens.option_d: That Jesus would lead the Jews out of Roman bondage
teens.explanation: John 11:50-51 — "it is expedient for us, that one man should die for the people, and that the whole nation perish not."

adults.question: Why did Jesus intentionally delay going to Bethany after hearing Lazarus was sick?
adults.option_a: He said "This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God" — and He stayed two more days
adults.option_b: He was occupied with teaching in the temple
adults.option_c: He was waiting for a sign from the Father
adults.option_d: He wanted to travel safely by night
adults.explanation: John 11:4,6 — "This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God… he abode two days still in the same place."

adults.question: What did Thomas say to the other disciples about going to Judaea with Jesus?
adults.option_a: "Let us also go, that we may die with him"
adults.option_b: "We must trust that the Father will protect us"
adults.option_c: "It is too dangerous; we should stay here"
adults.option_d: "Let us wait until the feast is over"
adults.explanation: John 11:16 — "Then said Thomas… Let us also go, that we may die with him."

adults.question: What did Martha say to Jesus that showed she still had faith despite Lazarus's death?
adults.option_a: "Even now, whatsoever thou wilt ask of God, God will give it thee"
adults.option_b: "If thou hadst been here, none of this would have happened"
adults.option_c: "I believe you can do all things"
adults.option_d: "The Father always hears your prayers"
adults.explanation: John 11:22 — "But I know, that even now, whatsoever thou wilt ask of God, God will give it thee."

adults.question: What practical objection did Martha raise when Jesus said to remove the stone from the tomb?
adults.option_a: "Lord, by this time he stinketh: for he hath been dead four days"
adults.option_b: "The stone is too heavy for us to move"
adults.option_c: "It is not lawful to open a tomb on this day"
adults.option_d: "The body has already been prepared for burial"
adults.explanation: John 11:39 — "Lord, by this time he stinketh: for he hath been dead four days."

adults.question: What did Jesus pray aloud at the tomb, and why?
adults.option_a: He thanked the Father for hearing Him, and said He spoke aloud so the people would believe the Father sent Him
adults.option_b: He asked the Father for power to raise the dead
adults.option_c: He recited a psalm of deliverance
adults.option_d: He called upon the angels to open the grave
adults.explanation: John 11:41-42 — "Father, I thank thee that thou hast heard me… because of the people which stand by I said it, that they may believe that thou hast sent me."

adults.question: According to John, what broader prophecy did Caiaphas unknowingly make beyond the Jewish nation?
adults.option_a: That Jesus would die not for the nation only, but to gather together in one the children of God scattered abroad
adults.option_b: That Jesus would establish a new covenant for all peoples
adults.option_c: That the temple would be destroyed and a spiritual one raised
adults.option_d: That the Gentiles would also receive the law of Moses
adults.explanation: John 11:52 — "not for that nation only, but that also he should gather together in one the children of God that were scattered abroad."

adults.question: What was the immediate political result of the raising of Lazarus?
adults.option_a: The chief priests and Pharisees convened a council and plotted to put Jesus to death
adults.option_b: The Romans sent soldiers to arrest Jesus
adults.option_c: Herod ordered Jesus brought before him
adults.option_d: The Sanhedrin declared Jesus a false prophet
adults.explanation: John 11:47,53 — "Then gathered the chief priests and the Pharisees a council… from that day forth they took counsel together for to put him to death."

adults.question: Where did Jesus withdraw to after the council plotted against Him?
adults.option_a: A city called Ephraim near the wilderness, with His disciples
adults.option_b: Galilee, to the region of Capernaum
adults.option_c: Beyond Jordan where John had baptized
adults.option_d: The desert of Judaea for forty days
adults.explanation: John 11:54 — "Jesus… went thence unto a country near to the wilderness, into a city called Ephraim, and there continued with his disciples."

kids.question: What did Mary pour on Jesus' feet?
kids.explanation: John 12:3 — "Mary took a pound of ointment of spikenard, very costly, and anointed the feet of Jesus."

kids.question: What animal did Jesus ride into Jerusalem?
kids.explanation: John 12:14 — "Jesus, when he had found a young ass, sat thereon."

kids.question: What did the crowd wave and shout when Jesus entered Jerusalem?
kids.explanation: John 12:13 — "Took branches of palm trees, and went forth to meet him, and cried, Hosanna."

teens.question: Who complained about the expensive ointment being wasted on Jesus?
teens.option_a: Judas Iscariot
teens.option_b: Peter
teens.option_c: Thomas
teens.explanation: John 12:4-5 — "Then saith one of his disciples, Judas Iscariot… Why was not this ointment sold?"

teens.question: What was the real reason Judas complained about the ointment?
teens.option_a: He was a thief and held the money bag
teens.option_b: He thought the money should go to the temple
teens.option_c: He was jealous of Mary's devotion
teens.option_d: He genuinely cared about the poor
teens.explanation: John 12:6 — "he was a thief, and had the bag, and bare what was put therein."

teens.question: What illustration about grain did Jesus use to describe His coming death?
teens.option_a: "Except a corn of wheat fall into the ground and die, it abideth alone: but if it die, it bringeth forth much fruit"
teens.option_b: "The wheat and the tares grow together until the harvest"
teens.option_c: "A sower went forth to sow, and some fell on good ground"
teens.option_d: "First the blade, then the ear, then the full corn"
teens.explanation: John 12:24 — "Except a corn of wheat fall into the ground and die, it abideth alone: but if it die, it bringeth forth much fruit."

teens.question: Who came seeking Jesus during the Passover feast?
teens.option_a: Certain Greeks who wanted to see Jesus
teens.option_b: Roman soldiers looking for a healer
teens.option_c: Samaritan pilgrims seeking a prophet
teens.option_d: Egyptian travelers who heard of His fame
teens.explanation: John 12:20-21 — "certain Greeks… came… saying, Sir, we would see Jesus."

teens.question: What did the voice from heaven say when Jesus prayed?
teens.option_a: "I have both glorified it, and will glorify it again"
teens.option_b: "This is my beloved Son, hear him"
teens.option_c: "I am well pleased with thee"
teens.explanation: John 12:28 — "Then came there a voice from heaven, saying, I have both glorified it, and will glorify it again."

adults.question: How much was the ointment of spikenard worth, according to Judas?
adults.option_a: Three hundred pence (about a year's wages)
adults.option_b: One hundred pence
adults.option_c: Fifty pence
adults.option_d: Five hundred pence
adults.explanation: John 12:5 — "Why was not this ointment sold for three hundred pence?"

adults.question: What did Jesus say Mary's anointing was for?
adults.option_a: "Against the day of my burying hath she kept this"
adults.option_b: "She has done this as an act of worship"
adults.option_c: "She anoints me as king of Israel"
adults.option_d: "This is a sign of the coming kingdom"
adults.explanation: John 12:7 — "against the day of my burying hath she kept this."

adults.question: Why did the chief priests also plan to put Lazarus to death?
adults.option_a: Because many Jews believed on Jesus because of Lazarus's resurrection
adults.option_b: Because Lazarus was preaching about Jesus
adults.option_c: Because Lazarus challenged the authority of the priests
adults.option_d: Because Lazarus refused to return to the synagogue
adults.explanation: John 12:10-11 — "the chief priests consulted that they might put Lazarus also to death; Because that by reason of him many of the Jews… believed on Jesus."

adults.question: What Old Testament prophecy did the crowd's actions at the triumphal entry fulfill?
adults.option_a: "Fear not, daughter of Sion: behold, thy King cometh, sitting on an ass's colt" (Zechariah 9:9)
adults.option_b: "Behold, I send my messenger before thy face" (Malachi 3:1)
adults.option_c: "The sceptre shall not depart from Judah" (Genesis 49:10)
adults.option_d: "A star shall come out of Jacob" (Numbers 24:17)
adults.explanation: John 12:15 — "Fear not, daughter of Sion: behold, thy King cometh, sitting on an ass's colt."

adults.question: What did Jesus say about what would happen when He was "lifted up from the earth"?
adults.option_a: "I, if I be lifted up from the earth, will draw all men unto me"
adults.option_b: "The heavens shall open and the angels descend"
adults.option_c: "The temple shall be destroyed and rebuilt"
adults.option_d: "All nations shall come and worship before me"
adults.explanation: John 12:32 — "And I, if I be lifted up from the earth, will draw all men unto me."

adults.question: What does John say about why many rulers who believed in Jesus did not confess Him?
adults.option_a: They loved the praise of men more than the praise of God, and feared being put out of the synagogue
adults.option_b: They did not yet fully understand His teaching
adults.option_c: They were waiting for the right political moment
adults.option_d: They feared Roman retaliation
adults.explanation: John 12:42-43 — "because of the Pharisees they did not confess him, lest they should be put out of the synagogue: For they loved the praise of men more than the praise of God."

adults.question: What did Jesus say about the purpose of the voice from heaven?
adults.option_a: "This voice came not because of me, but for your sakes"
adults.option_b: "The Father speaks so that you may fear Him"
adults.option_c: "This voice confirms my authority as the Son"
adults.option_d: "The voice is a sign of the coming judgment"
adults.explanation: John 12:30 — "This voice came not because of me, but for your sakes."

adults.question: What prophecy of Isaiah does John quote to explain the Jews' unbelief?
adults.option_a: "He hath blinded their eyes, and hardened their heart" (Isaiah 6:10, also Isaiah 53:1)
adults.option_b: "The people that walked in darkness have seen a great light" (Isaiah 9:2)
adults.option_c: "Who hath believed our report?" (Isaiah 53:1)
adults.option_d: "Hear ye indeed, but understand not" (Isaiah 6:9)
adults.explanation: John 12:38-40 — John quotes both Isaiah 53:1 ("Lord, who hath believed our report?") and Isaiah 6:10 ("He hath blinded their eyes, and hardened their heart"). The primary verse cited is Isaiah 53:1.

kids.question: What did Jesus do for His disciples at the Last Supper?
kids.explanation: John 13:5 — Jesus "began to wash the disciples' feet."

kids.question: What new commandment did Jesus give His disciples?
kids.explanation: John 13:34 — "A new commandment I give unto you, That ye love one another."

kids.question: Who did Jesus say would betray Him?
kids.option_a: Judas (the one who received the piece of bread)
kids.explanation: John 13:26 — Jesus gave the sop to Judas Iscariot, identifying him as the betrayer.

teens.question: Which disciple first refused to let Jesus wash his feet?
teens.option_a: Peter
teens.option_b: James
teens.option_c: John
teens.explanation: John 13:6-8 — Peter said "Thou shalt never wash my feet."

teens.question: What did Jesus say to Peter when he refused the foot-washing?
teens.option_a: "If I wash thee not, thou hast no part with me"
teens.option_b: "You must obey your Master"
teens.option_c: "This is a command, not a request"
teens.explanation: John 13:8 — "If I wash thee not, thou hast no part with me."

teens.question: How did Jesus identify who would betray Him?
teens.option_a: He dipped a piece of bread (sop) and gave it to Judas
teens.option_b: He pointed directly at Judas
teens.option_c: He whispered the name to Peter
teens.option_d: He said the betrayer's name aloud
teens.explanation: John 13:26 — "He it is, to whom I shall give a sop, when I have dipped it."

teens.question: What did Jesus say to Judas after giving him the bread?
teens.option_a: "That thou doest, do quickly"
teens.option_b: "Leave this place and never return"
teens.option_c: "You will regret this betrayal"
teens.explanation: John 13:27 — "That thou doest, do quickly."

teens.question: What did Jesus say people would know His disciples by?
teens.option_a: By their love for one another
teens.option_b: By their great miracles
teens.option_c: By their knowledge of Scripture
teens.option_d: By their obedience to the law
teens.explanation: John 13:35 — "By this shall all men know that ye are my disciples, if ye have love one to another."

adults.question: What does John 13:1 say about Jesus' knowledge as the Passover approached?
adults.option_a: Jesus knew His hour was come to depart out of this world unto the Father
adults.option_b: Jesus knew the Pharisees were planning His arrest
adults.option_c: Jesus knew He must go to the garden to pray
adults.option_d: Jesus knew the disciples would all forsake Him
adults.explanation: John 13:1 — "Jesus knew that his hour was come that he should depart out of this world unto the Father."

adults.question: What had the devil already done before the foot-washing, according to John?
adults.option_a: Put into the heart of Judas Iscariot to betray Jesus
adults.option_b: Tempted Peter to deny Jesus
adults.option_c: Scattered the disciples in fear
adults.option_d: Entered the Sanhedrin to plot Jesus' death
adults.explanation: John 13:2 — "the devil having now put into the heart of Judas Iscariot, Simon's son, to betray him."

adults.question: After Peter changed his mind and asked for a full washing, what did Jesus say?
adults.option_a: "He that is washed needeth not save to wash his feet, but is clean every whit"
adults.option_b: "I will wash you from head to toe as you wish"
adults.option_c: "Only your hands and feet need washing"
adults.option_d: "The one who is clean needs no washing at all"
adults.explanation: John 13:10 — "He that is washed needeth not save to wash his feet, but is clean every whit."

adults.question: What lesson did Jesus draw from the foot-washing?
adults.option_a: "If I then, your Lord and Master, have washed your feet; ye also ought to wash one another's feet"
adults.option_b: "A servant is greater than his master when he serves"
adults.option_c: "Cleanliness is next to godliness"
adults.option_d: "The last shall be first in the kingdom"
adults.explanation: John 13:14 — "If I then, your Lord and Master, have washed your feet; ye also ought to wash one another's feet."

adults.question: What Scripture did Jesus say was fulfilled by His betrayal?
adults.option_a: "He that eateth bread with me hath lifted up his heel against me" (Psalm 41:9)
adults.option_b: "They weighed for my price thirty pieces of silver" (Zechariah 11:12)
adults.option_c: "My own familiar friend hath dealt treacherously" (Psalm 55:12-13)
adults.option_d: "He was numbered with the transgressors" (Isaiah 53:12)
adults.explanation: John 13:18 — "He that eateth bread with me hath lifted up his heel against me."

adults.question: Which disciple was leaning on Jesus' bosom at supper?
adults.option_a: The disciple whom Jesus loved (traditionally identified as John)
adults.option_b: Peter
adults.option_c: Andrew
adults.option_d: James
adults.explanation: John 13:23 — "there was leaning on Jesus' bosom one of his disciples, whom Jesus loved."

adults.question: What did the other disciples think Judas was leaving to do?
adults.option_a: Buy things needed for the feast or give something to the poor (since he had the money bag)
adults.option_b: Go pray at the temple
adults.option_c: Fetch more wine for the supper
adults.option_d: Meet with the chief priests about a legitimate matter
adults.explanation: John 13:29 — "some of them thought… that Judas had the bag, that Jesus had said unto him, Buy those things that we have need of against the feast; or, that he should give something to the poor."

adults.question: What did Jesus predict about Peter at the end of chapter 13?
adults.option_a: Peter would deny Him three times before the cock crowed
adults.option_b: Peter would betray Him to the Pharisees
adults.option_c: Peter would fall asleep during His hour of trial
adults.option_d: Peter would flee to Galilee that night
adults.explanation: John 13:38 — "The cock shall not crow, till thou hast denied me thrice."

kids.question: What did Jesus say He was going to prepare for His disciples?
kids.option_a: A place in His Father's house (many mansions)
