kids.explanation: John 14:2 — "In my Father's house are many mansions… I go to prepare a place for you."

kids.question: What three things did Jesus say He is?
kids.option_a: The way, the truth, and the life
kids.option_b: The bread, the water, and the light
kids.option_c: The king, the priest, and the prophet
kids.option_d: The father, the son, and the spirit
kids.explanation: John 14:6 — "I am the way, the truth, and the life."

kids.question: What helper did Jesus promise to send to His disciples?
kids.explanation: John 14:26 — "the Comforter, which is the Holy Ghost."

teens.question: According to Jesus, how can a person come to the Father?
teens.option_a: Only through Jesus — "no man cometh unto the Father, but by me"
teens.option_b: Through keeping the law of Moses
teens.option_c: Through the prayers of the priests
teens.explanation: John 14:6 — "no man cometh unto the Father, but by me."

teens.question: What did Jesus say to comfort His disciples about His departure?
teens.option_a: "Let not your heart be troubled: ye believe in God, believe also in me"
teens.option_b: "Do not weep, for I will return tomorrow"
teens.option_c: "Be strong and carry on my work"
teens.option_d: "The world will treat you well after I go"
teens.explanation: John 14:1 — "Let not your heart be troubled: ye believe in God, believe also in me."

teens.question: What did Jesus promise the Comforter (Holy Spirit) would do?
teens.option_a: Teach them all things and bring to remembrance all that Jesus had said
teens.option_b: Give them power to perform great miracles
teens.option_c: Make them rulers over Israel
teens.option_d: Protect them from all physical harm
teens.explanation: John 14:26 — "he shall teach you all things, and bring all things to your remembrance, whatsoever I have said unto you."

teens.question: What did Philip ask Jesus?
teens.option_a: "Lord, shew us the Father, and it sufficeth us"
teens.option_b: "Lord, teach us to pray"
teens.option_c: "Lord, how can we know the way?"
teens.option_d: "Lord, who is the greatest among us?"
teens.explanation: John 14:8 — "Philip saith unto him, Lord, shew us the Father, and it sufficeth us."

teens.question: What kind of peace did Jesus say He gives, different from the world's?
teens.option_a: "My peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you"
teens.option_b: "I give you peace through prosperity and success"
teens.option_c: "I give you peace by removing all your enemies"
teens.option_d: "I give you the peace of never having to work again"
teens.explanation: John 14:27 — "Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you."

adults.question: What did Jesus say about the relationship between seeing Him and seeing the Father?
adults.option_a: "He that hath seen me hath seen the Father" — the Father dwells in Him
adults.option_b: The Father is invisible and cannot be seen through any means
adults.option_c: The Father will appear separately at the end of time
adults.option_d: One must die first to see the Father face to face
adults.explanation: John 14:9 — "he that hath seen me hath seen the Father."

adults.question: What does Jesus say about the works His believers will do?
adults.option_a: They shall do the works He does, and greater works, because He goes to the Father
adults.option_b: They shall do lesser works as humble servants
adults.option_c: They shall only teach and preach, not do miracles
adults.option_d: They shall do the same works but nothing greater
adults.explanation: John 14:12 — "the works that I do shall he do also; and greater works than these shall he do; because I go unto my Father."

adults.question: What condition does Jesus give for answered prayer in this chapter?
adults.option_a: "If ye shall ask any thing in my name, I will do it"
adults.option_b: "If you fast and pray with a pure heart"
adults.option_c: "If you first reconcile with your brother"
adults.option_d: "If you pray in the temple at the appointed hour"
adults.explanation: John 14:14 — "If ye shall ask any thing in my name, I will do it."

adults.question: What does Jesus call the Holy Spirit in John 14:16-17?
adults.option_a: Another Comforter — the Spirit of truth, whom the world cannot receive
adults.option_b: The Spirit of power that will come upon them
adults.option_c: The angel of the Lord who will guard them
adults.option_d: The spirit of prophecy that will reveal the future
adults.explanation: John 14:16-17 — "another Comforter… even the Spirit of truth; whom the world cannot receive."

adults.question: What did Jesus say about the person who loves Him regarding the Father?
adults.option_a: "He that loveth me shall be loved of my Father, and I will love him, and will manifest myself to him"
adults.option_b: "He shall sit at the right hand of the Father"
adults.option_c: "He shall be called a friend of God"
adults.option_d: "He shall never face judgment"
adults.explanation: John 14:21 — "he that loveth me shall be loved of my Father, and I will love him, and will manifest myself to him."

adults.question: What question did Judas (not Iscariot) ask Jesus?
adults.option_a: "Lord, how is it that thou wilt manifest thyself unto us, and not unto the world?"
adults.option_b: "Lord, when will thou restore the kingdom to Israel?"
adults.option_c: "Lord, which of us is the greatest?"
adults.option_d: "Lord, shall we call fire down from heaven?"
adults.explanation: John 14:22 — "Judas saith unto him, not Iscariot, Lord, how is it that thou wilt manifest thyself unto us, and not unto the world?"

adults.question: What does Jesus say about His relationship to the Father near the end of the chapter?
adults.option_a: "The Father is greater than I"
adults.option_b: "The Father and I are the same being"
adults.option_c: "The Father has given me all His authority permanently"
adults.option_d: "The Father works through me but we are separate"
adults.explanation: John 14:28 — "my Father is greater than I."

adults.question: What reason does Jesus give for telling His disciples about His departure beforehand?
adults.option_a: "I have told you before it come to pass, that, when it is come to pass, ye might believe"
adults.option_b: "So that you can prepare yourselves for tribulation"
adults.option_c: "So that you can tell others what will happen"
adults.option_d: "So that you will not be afraid of the Romans"
adults.explanation: John 14:29 — "now I have told you before it come to pass, that, when it is come to pass, ye might believe."

kids.question: What did Jesus compare Himself to in this chapter?
kids.option_a: A vine — "I am the true vine"
kids.option_b: A tree — "I am the tallest tree"
kids.option_c: A flower — "I am the beautiful rose"
kids.option_d: A river — "I am the flowing river"
kids.explanation: John 15:1 — "I am the true vine."

kids.question: What word did Jesus repeat many times, telling His disciples to stay connected to Him?
kids.explanation: John 15:4 — "Abide in me, and I in you."

kids.question: What is the greatest kind of love, according to Jesus?
kids.option_a: When someone gives up their life for their friends
kids.option_b: When someone gives all their money away
kids.option_c: When someone is nice to strangers
kids.option_d: When someone prays all day long
kids.explanation: John 15:13 — "Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends."

teens.question: In the vine and branches metaphor, who is the farmer (husbandman)?
teens.option_a: The Father
teens.option_b: Jesus
teens.option_c: The Holy Spirit
teens.explanation: John 15:1 — "I am the true vine, and my Father is the husbandman."

teens.question: What happens to branches that do not bear fruit?
teens.option_a: They are taken away; branches that bear fruit are pruned to bear more
teens.option_b: They are watered more until they produce
teens.option_c: They are replanted in better soil
teens.option_d: They are left alone to grow naturally
teens.explanation: John 15:2 — "Every branch in me that beareth not fruit he taketh away: and every branch that beareth fruit, he purgeth it, that it may bring forth more fruit."

teens.question: What did Jesus say about doing things without Him?
teens.option_a: "Without me ye can do nothing"
teens.option_b: "Without me ye will struggle but still succeed"
teens.option_c: "Without me ye must rely on the law"
teens.option_d: "Without me ye must find another teacher"
teens.explanation: John 15:5 — "without me ye can do nothing."

teens.question: What did Jesus call His disciples instead of servants?
teens.option_a: Friends — "I have called you friends"
teens.option_b: Brothers — "I have called you brothers"
teens.option_c: Sons — "I have called you sons"
teens.option_d: Chosen ones — "I have called you chosen"
teens.explanation: John 15:15 — "I have called you friends."

teens.question: Why did Jesus say the world would hate His disciples?
teens.option_a: Because they are not of the world, just as Jesus is not of the world
teens.option_b: Because they would perform miracles the world envied
teens.option_c: Because they would become wealthy and powerful
teens.option_d: Because they would refuse to pay Roman taxes
teens.explanation: John 15:19 — "because ye are not of the world, but I have chosen you out of the world, therefore the world hateth you."

adults.question: What condition does Jesus give for effective prayer in the vine discourse?
adults.option_a: "If ye abide in me, and my words abide in you, ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you"
adults.option_b: "If ye fast and pray continually, your requests will be granted"
adults.option_c: "If ye keep all the commandments, the Father will hear you"
adults.option_d: "If ye pray in the temple, your prayers will be answered"
adults.explanation: John 15:7 — "If ye abide in me, and my words abide in you, ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you."

adults.question: What does Jesus say is the evidence that proves we are His disciples?
adults.option_a: "Herein is my Father glorified, that ye bear much fruit; so shall ye be my disciples"
adults.option_b: "By your knowledge of the Scriptures, men will know you are mine"
adults.option_c: "By your miracles, men will know the Father sent me"
adults.option_d: "By your obedience to every commandment"
adults.explanation: John 15:8 — "Herein is my Father glorified, that ye bear much fruit; so shall ye be my disciples."

adults.question: Why did Jesus say He no longer calls His disciples servants?
adults.option_a: "The servant knoweth not what his lord doeth: but I have called you friends; for all things that I have heard of my Father I have made known unto you"
adults.option_b: "Because you have earned the title of friend through your loyalty"
adults.option_c: "Because servants cannot enter the kingdom of heaven"
adults.option_d: "Because the servant's role has ended now that the Messiah has come"
adults.explanation: John 15:15 — "the servant knoweth not what his lord doeth: but I have called you friends; for all things that I have heard of my Father I have made known unto you."

adults.question: What principle did Jesus state about the relationship between hatred toward Him and hatred toward the Father?
adults.option_a: "He that hateth me hateth my Father also"
adults.option_b: "Those who hate me will eventually come to love the Father"
adults.option_c: "Hatred of me does not affect one's standing before the Father"
adults.option_d: "The Father will judge those who hate me at the last day"
adults.explanation: John 15:23 — "He that hateth me hateth my Father also."

adults.question: What does Jesus say about the guilt of those who have seen His works?
adults.option_a: "If I had not done among them the works which none other man did, they had not had sin: but now have they both seen and hated both me and my Father"
adults.option_b: "Their guilt is lessened because they did not understand"
adults.option_c: "They will be forgiven because they acted in ignorance"
adults.option_d: "Their sin would be the same whether or not they saw the works"
adults.explanation: John 15:24 — "If I had not done among them the works which none other man did, they had not had sin."

adults.question: What Old Testament Scripture does Jesus say is fulfilled by the hatred against Him?
adults.option_a: "They hated me without a cause" (Psalm 35:19 / Psalm 69:4)
adults.option_b: "The stone which the builders rejected" (Psalm 118:22)
adults.option_c: "He was despised and rejected of men" (Isaiah 53:3)
adults.option_d: "They pierced my hands and my feet" (Psalm 22:16)
adults.explanation: John 15:25 — "But this cometh to pass, that the word might be fulfilled that is written in their law, They hated me without a cause."

adults.question: What does Jesus say the Comforter will do when He comes?
adults.option_a: "He shall testify of me" — and the disciples shall also bear witness
adults.option_b: "He shall judge the world and convict sinners"
adults.option_c: "He shall establish the church in Jerusalem"
adults.option_d: "He shall write new scriptures for the people"
adults.explanation: John 15:26-27 — "he shall testify of me: And ye also shall bear witness."

adults.question: What is the commandment Jesus specifically ties to the vine metaphor about love?
adults.option_a: "This is my commandment, That ye love one another, as I have loved you"
adults.option_b: "Love the Lord thy God with all thy heart"
adults.option_c: "Love thy neighbour as thyself"
adults.option_d: "Love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you"
adults.explanation: John 15:12 — "This is my commandment, That ye love one another, as I have loved you."

kids.question: Who did Jesus promise to send after He went away?
kids.explanation: John 16:7 — "if I depart, I will send him [the Comforter] unto you."

kids.question: What did Jesus say about sadness turning into something else?
kids.option_a: Sorrow would be turned into joy
kids.option_b: Sorrow would be turned into sleep
kids.option_c: Sorrow would be turned into work
kids.option_d: Sorrow would be turned into silence
kids.explanation: John 16:20 — "your sorrow shall be turned into joy."

kids.question: What did Jesus say He had done to the world to encourage His disciples?
kids.option_a: "Be of good cheer; I have overcome the world"
kids.option_b: "Be of good cheer; I have rebuilt the world"
kids.option_c: "Be of good cheer; I have hidden from the world"
kids.option_d: "Be of good cheer; I have escaped the world"
kids.explanation: John 16:33 — "be of good cheer; I have overcome the world."

teens.question: What three things did Jesus say the Spirit of truth would convict the world of?
teens.option_a: Sin, righteousness, and judgment
teens.option_b: Greed, pride, and hatred
teens.option_c: Lying, stealing, and murder
teens.explanation: John 16:8 — "he will reprove the world of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment."

teens.question: What did Jesus say the Spirit of truth would do when He came?
teens.option_a: "He will guide you into all truth" and show things to come
teens.option_b: He would give them power to defeat Rome
teens.option_c: He would make them invisible to their enemies
teens.option_d: He would write new laws for them to follow
teens.explanation: John 16:13 — "when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will guide you into all truth… and he will shew you things to come."

teens.question: What comparison did Jesus use for the disciples' sorrow and joy?
teens.option_a: A woman in labor — she has sorrow during childbirth but joy when the child is born
teens.option_b: A farmer waiting for harvest — sorrow in planting, joy in reaping
teens.option_c: A student studying — sorrow in learning, joy in passing the test
teens.option_d: A soldier in battle — sorrow in fighting, joy in victory
teens.explanation: John 16:21 — "A woman when she is in travail hath sorrow… but as soon as she is delivered of the child, she remembereth no more the anguish, for joy."

teens.question: What phrase did Jesus keep using about a short time?
teens.option_a: "A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me"
teens.option_b: "For a moment I leave, but I will return forever"
teens.option_c: "In a short season the kingdom will come"
teens.option_d: "Before the sun sets twice, I will appear again"
teens.explanation: John 16:16 — "A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me."

teens.question: What warning did Jesus give about what would happen to His followers?
teens.option_a: They would be put out of the synagogues, and some would be killed by those who thought they were serving God
teens.option_b: They would be imprisoned by the Romans
teens.option_c: They would lose all their money and possessions
teens.option_d: They would be scattered to foreign lands
teens.explanation: John 16:2 — "They shall put you out of the synagogues: yea, the time cometh, that whosoever killeth you will think that he doeth God service."

adults.question: Why did Jesus say it was "expedient" (advantageous) for Him to go away?
adults.option_a: "If I go not away, the Comforter will not come unto you; but if I depart, I will send him unto you"
adults.option_b: "So that you will learn to stand on your own faith"
adults.option_c: "Because the Father requires me to return before sending help"
adults.option_d: "So that the world will no longer persecute you through me"
adults.explanation: John 16:7 — "if I go not away, the Comforter will not come unto you; but if I depart, I will send him unto you."

adults.question: How does Jesus explain the Spirit's conviction concerning sin?
adults.option_a: "Of sin, because they believe not on me"
adults.option_b: "Of sin, because they have broken the law"
adults.option_c: "Of sin, because they have rejected the prophets"
adults.option_d: "Of sin, because they worship false gods"
adults.explanation: John 16:9 — "Of sin, because they believe not on me."

adults.question: How does Jesus explain the Spirit's conviction concerning righteousness?
adults.option_a: "Of righteousness, because I go to my Father, and ye see me no more"
adults.option_b: "Of righteousness, because the law demands perfection"
adults.option_c: "Of righteousness, because God is holy"
adults.option_d: "Of righteousness, because the prophets spoke of justice"
adults.explanation: John 16:10 — "Of righteousness, because I go to my Father, and ye see me no more."

adults.question: How does Jesus explain the Spirit's conviction concerning judgment?
adults.option_a: "Of judgment, because the prince of this world is judged"
adults.option_b: "Of judgment, because the day of the Lord is near"
adults.option_c: "Of judgment, because all must appear before God's throne"
adults.option_d: "Of judgment, because the wicked will be destroyed"
adults.explanation: John 16:11 — "Of judgment, because the prince of this world is judged."

adults.question: What does Jesus say about the Spirit's source of teaching?
adults.option_a: "He shall not speak of himself; but whatsoever he shall hear, that shall he speak"
adults.option_b: "He shall bring new revelation unknown to the prophets"
adults.option_c: "He shall speak only what the disciples ask Him"
adults.option_d: "He shall interpret the law of Moses in a new way"
adults.explanation: John 16:13 — "he shall not speak of himself; but whatsoever he shall hear, that shall he speak."

adults.question: What does Jesus say the Spirit will do regarding Jesus Himself?
adults.option_a: "He shall glorify me: for he shall receive of mine, and shall shew it unto you"
adults.option_b: "He shall establish my kingdom on earth in my absence"
adults.option_c: "He shall remind you of Moses and the prophets"
adults.option_d: "He shall give you power to continue my miracles"
adults.explanation: John 16:14 — "He shall glorify me: for he shall receive of mine, and shall shew it unto you."

adults.question: What promise about prayer does Jesus make in John 16:23-24?
adults.option_a: "Whatsoever ye shall ask the Father in my name, he will give it you… ask, and ye shall receive, that your joy may be full"
adults.option_b: "Pray without ceasing and the Father will hear every word"
adults.option_c: "If two or three agree, it shall be done"
adults.option_d: "Whatever you bind on earth shall be bound in heaven"
adults.explanation: John 16:23-24 — "Whatsoever ye shall ask the Father in my name, he will give it you… ask, and ye shall receive, that your joy may be full."

adults.question: What did the disciples finally say they now understood about Jesus?
adults.option_a: "Now are we sure that thou knowest all things… by this we believe that thou camest forth from God"
adults.option_b: "We now understand all your parables and teachings"
adults.option_c: "We believe you are the prophet Moses foretold"
adults.option_d: "We know you will defeat Rome and restore Israel"
adults.explanation: John 16:30 — "Now are we sure that thou knowest all things… by this we believe that thou camest forth from God."

kids.question: What did Jesus do for His disciples in this whole chapter?
kids.option_b: He taught them a new song
kids.option_c: He fed them bread and fish
kids.explanation: John 17 is Jesus' prayer for His disciples (the High Priestly Prayer).

kids.question: What did Jesus ask the Father to do for His followers?
kids.option_a: Keep them safe and make them one (united)
kids.option_d: Send them on a long vacation
kids.explanation: John 17:11 — "Holy Father, keep through thine own name those whom thou hast given me, that they may be one."

kids.question: Did Jesus pray only for His disciples, or for others too?
kids.option_a: He also prayed for all future believers
kids.option_b: He prayed only for the twelve disciples
kids.option_c: He prayed only for the people of Israel
kids.explanation: John 17:20 — "Neither pray I for these alone, but for them also which shall believe on me through their word."

teens.question: How did Jesus define eternal life in His prayer?
teens.option_a: "That they might know thee the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom thou hast sent"
teens.option_b: "That they might live forever in a perfect world"
teens.option_c: "That they might have everlasting health and wealth"
teens.option_d: "That they might keep all the commandments perfectly"
teens.explanation: John 17:3 — "this is life eternal, that they might know thee the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom thou hast sent."

teens.question: What did Jesus say He had given to His disciples from the Father?
teens.option_a: The Father's words — and they received them and believed
teens.option_b: The Father's crown and kingdom
teens.option_c: The Father's written law
teens.explanation: John 17:8 — "I have given unto them the words which thou gavest me; and they have received them."

teens.question: What did Jesus ask the Father to protect His disciples from?
teens.option_a: The evil one (the devil)
teens.option_b: The Roman soldiers
teens.option_c: Physical sickness and death
teens.explanation: John 17:15 — "I pray not that thou shouldest take them out of the world, but that thou shouldest keep them from the evil."

teens.question: What did Jesus ask the Father to do for believers through His truth?
teens.option_a: "Sanctify them through thy truth: thy word is truth"
teens.option_b: "Teach them new commandments"
teens.option_c: "Give them power over their enemies"
teens.explanation: John 17:17 — "Sanctify them through thy truth: thy word is truth."

teens.question: What was Jesus' desire for all believers to see?
teens.option_a: His glory which the Father gave Him before the foundation of the world
teens.option_b: The new Jerusalem coming down from heaven
teens.option_c: The angels worshipping around God's throne
teens.option_d: The destruction of all evil in the world
teens.explanation: John 17:24 — "I will that they also, whom thou hast given me, be with me where I am; that they may behold my glory, which thou hast given me: for thou lovedst me before the foundation of the world."

adults.question: What does Jesus claim He has done with the Father's name?
adults.option_a: "I have manifested thy name unto the men which thou gavest me out of the world"
adults.option_b: "I have written thy name upon their hearts"
adults.option_c: "I have proclaimed thy name in the temple"
adults.option_d: "I have revealed thy name to all the nations"
adults.explanation: John 17:6 — "I have manifested thy name unto the men which thou gavest me out of the world."

adults.question: What does Jesus say about the one who was lost among the twelve?
adults.option_a: "None of them is lost, but the son of perdition; that the scripture might be fulfilled"
adults.option_b: "One was taken by the enemy against my will"
adults.option_c: "One chose the path of destruction despite many warnings"
adults.option_d: "One fell away because his faith was weak"
adults.explanation: John 17:12 — "none of them is lost, but the son of perdition; that the scripture might be fulfilled."

adults.question: What does Jesus pray regarding the unity of believers?
adults.option_a: "That they all may be one; as thou, Father, art in me, and I in thee, that they also may be one in us"
adults.option_b: "That they may agree on all matters of doctrine"
adults.option_c: "That they may form one visible organization"
adults.option_d: "That they may never argue or disagree"
adults.explanation: John 17:21 — "That they all may be one; as thou, Father, art in me, and I in thee, that they also may be one in us."

adults.question: What glory does Jesus say He shared with the disciples?
adults.option_a: "The glory which thou gavest me I have given them; that they may be one, even as we are one"
adults.option_b: "The glory of performing miracles in my name"
adults.option_c: "The glory of ruling over the nations"
adults.option_d: "The glory of being called sons of God"
adults.explanation: John 17:22 — "the glory which thou gavest me I have given them; that they may be one, even as we are one."

adults.question: What does Jesus claim about the world's knowledge of the Father?
adults.option_a: "O righteous Father, the world hath not known thee: but I have known thee, and these have known that thou hast sent me"
adults.option_b: "The world knows thee through creation but rejects thy Son"
adults.option_c: "The world worships thee but does not understand thy ways"
adults.option_d: "The world will come to know thee through the testimony of the disciples"
adults.explanation: John 17:25 — "O righteous Father, the world hath not known thee: but I have known thee, and these have known that thou hast sent me."

adults.question: What was Jesus' stated purpose for being sent into the world regarding the disciples?
adults.option_a: "As thou hast sent me into the world, even so have I also sent them into the world"
adults.option_b: "I came to gather them from every nation"
adults.option_c: "I came to establish a new priesthood among them"
adults.option_d: "I came to teach them the mysteries of the kingdom"
adults.explanation: John 17:18 — "As thou hast sent me into the world, even so have I also sent them into the world."

adults.question: What does Jesus say He does for the disciples' sake regarding sanctification?
adults.option_a: "For their sakes I sanctify myself, that they also might be sanctified through the truth"
adults.option_b: "I have given them the law so they can purify themselves"
adults.option_c: "I have appointed priests to sanctify them on my behalf"
adults.option_d: "I have set them apart by removing them from the world"
adults.explanation: John 17:19 — "for their sakes I sanctify myself, that they also might be sanctified through the truth."

adults.question: What is the ultimate purpose of believers' unity, according to Jesus' prayer?
adults.option_a: "That the world may believe that thou hast sent me" and "that the world may know that thou hast sent me"
adults.option_b: "That the church may be strong against persecution"
adults.option_c: "That disputes among believers may cease"
adults.option_d: "That the gospel may be preached more effectively"
adults.explanation: John 17:21,23 — "that the world may believe that thou hast sent me" and "that the world may know that thou hast sent me."

kids.question: Who betrayed Jesus and brought soldiers to arrest Him?
kids.explanation: John 18:2-3 — "Judas… having received a band of men and officers… cometh thither."

kids.question: What did Peter do when the soldiers came to arrest Jesus?
kids.option_a: He cut off a servant's ear with a sword
kids.option_c: He called down fire from heaven
kids.explanation: John 18:10 — "Simon Peter having a sword drew it, and smote the high priest's servant, and cut off his right ear."

kids.question: How many times did Peter say he did not know Jesus?
kids.explanation: John 18:17,25,27 — Peter denied Jesus three times.

teens.question: What happened when Jesus said "I am he" to the soldiers who came to arrest Him?
teens.option_a: They went backward and fell to the ground
teens.option_b: They dropped their weapons
teens.option_c: They ran away in fear
teens.explanation: John 18:6 — "As soon then as he had said unto them, I am he, they went backward, and fell to the ground."

teens.question: What was the name of the servant whose ear Peter cut off?
teens.option_a: Malchus
teens.option_b: Marcus
teens.option_c: Lazarus
teens.explanation: John 18:10 — "The servant's name was Malchus."

teens.question: What did Jesus say to Peter about the sword?
teens.option_a: "Put up thy sword into the sheath: the cup which my Father hath given me, shall I not drink it?"
teens.option_b: "Those who live by the sword will die by the sword"
teens.option_c: "I have no need of your protection"
teens.option_d: "Do not fight, for my kingdom is not of this world"
teens.explanation: John 18:11 — "Put up thy sword into the sheath: the cup which my Father hath given me, shall I not drink it?"

teens.question: Who was Jesus taken to first after His arrest?
teens.option_a: Annas, the father-in-law of the high priest Caiaphas
teens.option_b: Pontius Pilate the Roman governor
teens.option_c: King Herod
teens.explanation: John 18:13 — "led him away to Annas first; for he was father in law to Caiaphas."

teens.question: What did Pilate ask Jesus that received a famous answer about His kingdom?
teens.option_a: "Art thou the King of the Jews?" — Jesus said His kingdom is not of this world
teens.option_b: "Are you the Son of God?"
teens.option_c: "Have you broken the law of Moses?"
teens.option_d: "Do you claim to be greater than Caesar?"
teens.explanation: John 18:33,36 — "Art thou the King of the Jews?… My kingdom is not of this world."

adults.question: What is the name of the garden where Jesus was arrested?
adults.option_a: A garden across the brook Cedron (Kidron) — traditionally Gethsemane
adults.option_b: The Garden of Eden
adults.option_c: The garden near the Pool of Siloam
adults.option_d: The king's garden near the walls of Jerusalem
adults.explanation: John 18:1 — "he went forth with his disciples over the brook Cedron, where was a garden."

adults.question: What did Jesus request for His disciples at the moment of arrest?
adults.option_a: "If therefore ye seek me, let these go their way" — fulfilling His word that He lost none given to Him
adults.option_b: "Protect my disciples from the soldiers"
adults.option_c: "Let them return to their homes in peace"
adults.option_d: "Do not punish them for my sake"
adults.explanation: John 18:8-9 — "If therefore ye seek me, let these go their way: That the saying might be fulfilled, which he spake, Of them which thou gavest me have I lost none."

adults.question: Which disciple followed Jesus into the high priest's palace because he was known to the high priest?
adults.option_a: Another disciple (traditionally identified as John) who was known to the high priest and brought Peter in
adults.option_b: Nicodemus who was a Pharisee
adults.option_c: Joseph of Arimathaea
adults.option_d: Matthew the tax collector
adults.explanation: John 18:15-16 — "that other disciple was known unto the high priest, and went in… and brought in Peter."

adults.question: What did Jesus say when an officer struck Him for His answer to the high priest?
adults.option_a: "If I have spoken evil, bear witness of the evil: but if well, why smitest thou me?"
adults.option_b: "Father, forgive him, for he knows not what he does"
adults.option_c: "You have no right to strike the Son of God"
adults.option_d: "The Father will judge you for this"
adults.explanation: John 18:23 — "If I have spoken evil, bear witness of the evil: but if well, why smitest thou me?"

adults.question: What did Jesus tell Pilate about the nature of His kingdom?
adults.option_a: "My kingdom is not of this world: if my kingdom were of this world, then would my servants fight"
adults.option_b: "My kingdom shall overthrow all earthly kingdoms"
adults.option_c: "My kingdom is spiritual and has no earthly manifestation"
adults.option_d: "My kingdom will come after the Romans fall"
adults.explanation: John 18:36 — "My kingdom is not of this world: if my kingdom were of this world, then would my servants fight."

adults.question: What was Pilate's famous philosophical question to Jesus?
adults.option_a: "What is truth?"
adults.option_b: "What is justice?"
adults.option_c: "What is power?"
adults.option_d: "What is freedom?"
adults.explanation: John 18:38 — "Pilate saith unto him, What is truth?"

adults.question: Whom did the Jews choose to be released instead of Jesus?
adults.option_a: Barabbas, who was a robber
adults.option_b: A murderer named Simon
adults.option_c: A zealot named Judas
adults.option_d: A thief named Dysmas
adults.explanation: John 18:40 — "Not this man, but Barabbas. Now Barabbas was a robber."

adults.question: What detail does John give about Peter's third denial?
adults.option_a: A relative of Malchus (whose ear Peter had cut off) asked "Did not I see thee in the garden with him?" — and the cock crew immediately
adults.option_b: A servant girl recognized Peter by his Galilean accent
adults.option_c: The soldiers identified Peter by his sword
adults.option_d: The high priest himself questioned Peter directly
adults.explanation: John 18:26-27 — "One of the servants of the high priest, being his kinsman whose ear Peter cut off, saith, Did not I see thee in the garden with him? Peter then denied again: and immediately the cock crew."

kids.question: What was put on Jesus' head to hurt and mock Him?
kids.explanation: John 19:2 — "the soldiers platted a crown of thorns, and put it on his head."

kids.question: What did Jesus say just before He died on the cross?
kids.explanation: John 19:30 — "he said, It is finished: and he bowed his head, and gave up the ghost."

kids.question: Where was Jesus crucified?
kids.option_a: A place called Golgotha (the place of a skull)
kids.explanation: John 19:17 — "a place called the place of a skull, which is called in the Hebrew Golgotha."

teens.question: What words did Pilate say when he presented Jesus to the crowd?
teens.option_a: "Behold the man!"
teens.option_b: "Here is your king!"
teens.option_c: "See the prisoner!"
teens.explanation: John 19:5 — "Pilate saith unto them, Behold the man!"

teens.question: What was written on the sign (title) placed on Jesus' cross?
teens.option_a: "JESUS OF NAZARETH THE KING OF THE JEWS" in Hebrew, Greek, and Latin
teens.option_b: "This man claimed to be the Son of God"
teens.option_c: "The Messiah of Israel"
teens.explanation: John 19:19-20 — "JESUS OF NAZARETH THE KING OF THE JEWS… in Hebrew, and Greek, and Latin."

teens.question: What did the soldiers do with Jesus' coat?
teens.option_a: They cast lots for it because it was woven without a seam
teens.option_b: They tore it into four pieces
teens.option_c: They gave it to His mother
teens.option_d: They burned it as an offering
teens.explanation: John 19:23-24 — "the coat was without seam, woven from the top throughout… Let us not rend it, but cast lots."

teens.question: What came out of Jesus' side when a soldier pierced it with a spear?
teens.option_a: Blood and water
teens.option_b: Only blood
teens.option_c: Only water
teens.explanation: John 19:34 — "one of the soldiers with a spear pierced his side, and forthwith came there out blood and water."

teens.question: Who buried Jesus' body?
teens.option_a: Joseph of Arimathaea and Nicodemus
teens.option_b: Peter and John
teens.option_c: Mary Magdalene and Mary His mother
teens.explanation: John 19:38-39 — "Joseph of Arimathaea… and… Nicodemus."

adults.question: What did Pilate say to Jesus about his authority, and how did Jesus respond?
adults.option_a: Pilate said he had power to crucify or release; Jesus said "Thou couldest have no power at all against me, except it were given thee from above"
adults.option_b: Pilate claimed Caesar's authority; Jesus said all authority is God's
adults.option_c: Pilate said Rome rules all; Jesus said the Father rules above Rome
adults.option_d: Pilate boasted of his rank; Jesus reminded him of his mortality
adults.explanation: John 19:10-11 — "Thou couldest have no power at all against me, except it were given thee from above."

adults.question: What argument did the Jews use to pressure Pilate into crucifying Jesus?
adults.option_a: "If thou let this man go, thou art not Caesar's friend: whosoever maketh himself a king speaketh against Caesar"
adults.option_b: "Our law says He must die for blasphemy"
adults.option_c: "He has incited rebellion against Rome"
adults.option_d: "The people will riot if you release Him"
adults.explanation: John 19:12 — "If thou let this man go, thou art not Caesar's friend."

adults.question: What did the chief priests say when Pilate asked "Shall I crucify your King?"
adults.option_a: "We have no king but Caesar"
adults.option_b: "He is not our king"
adults.option_c: "Crucify Him and give us Barabbas"
adults.option_d: "We reject this blasphemer"
adults.explanation: John 19:15 — "The chief priests answered, We have no king but Caesar."

adults.question: What did Jesus say to His mother and the beloved disciple from the cross?
adults.option_a: To His mother: "Woman, behold thy son!" To the disciple: "Behold thy mother!" — and the disciple took her to his own home
adults.option_b: "Take care of one another"
adults.option_c: "Remember me when I am gone"
adults.option_d: "Go to Galilee and wait for me"
adults.explanation: John 19:26-27 — "Woman, behold thy son!… Behold thy mother! And from that hour that disciple took her unto his own home."

adults.question: What Old Testament Scripture was fulfilled by the soldiers not breaking Jesus' bones?
adults.option_a: "A bone of him shall not be broken" (Exodus 12:46 / Psalm 34:20)
adults.option_b: "He keepeth all his bones: not one of them is broken" (Psalm 34:20)
adults.option_c: "They shall look on him whom they pierced" (Zechariah 12:10)
adults.option_d: "He was wounded for our transgressions" (Isaiah 53:5)
adults.explanation: John 19:36 — "A bone of him shall not be broken."

adults.question: What prophecy was fulfilled by the piercing of Jesus' side?
adults.option_a: "They shall look on him whom they pierced" (Zechariah 12:10)
adults.option_b: "A bone of him shall not be broken"
adults.option_c: "He was numbered with the transgressors" (Isaiah 53:12)
adults.option_d: "They parted my garments among them" (Psalm 22:18)
adults.explanation: John 19:37 — "And again another scripture saith, They shall look on him whom they pierced."

adults.question: How much spice did Nicodemus bring for Jesus' burial?
adults.option_a: A mixture of myrrh and aloes, about an hundred pound weight
adults.option_b: Frankincense and myrrh, about fifty pounds
adults.option_c: Pure nard and spices, about thirty pounds
adults.option_d: Aloes and cassia, about seventy pounds
adults.explanation: John 19:39 — "Nicodemus… brought a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about an hundred pound weight."

adults.question: What was Jesus' response when He knew all things were accomplished before He died?
adults.option_a: He said "I thirst" — and after receiving vinegar on hyssop, He said "It is finished"
adults.option_b: He cried out "My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?"
adults.option_c: He prayed silently and bowed His head
adults.option_d: He called out to His mother and then died
adults.explanation: John 19:28-30 — Jesus said "I thirst"… "It is finished: and he bowed his head, and gave up the ghost."

kids.question: Who came to Jesus' tomb early in the morning and found it empty?
kids.explanation: John 20:1 — "Mary Magdalene… cometh… unto the sepulchre, and seeth the stone taken away."

kids.question: Which disciple did not believe Jesus was alive until he saw Him?
kids.explanation: John 20:25 — Thomas said he would not believe unless he saw and touched Jesus' wounds.

kids.question: What did Jesus show His disciples to prove it was really Him?
kids.option_a: His hands and His side (where He was wounded)
kids.explanation: John 20:20 — "he shewed unto them his hands and his side."

teens.question: What did Mary Magdalene first think had happened to Jesus' body?
teens.option_a: Someone had taken the Lord out of the sepulchre
teens.option_b: He had risen as He predicted
teens.option_c: The soldiers had moved Him to another tomb
teens.option_d: The Pharisees had stolen His body
teens.explanation: John 20:2 — "They have taken away the Lord out of the sepulchre, and we know not where they have laid him."

teens.question: Who reached the tomb first — Peter or the other disciple?
teens.option_a: The other disciple outran Peter and reached the tomb first
teens.option_b: Peter reached the tomb first
teens.option_c: They arrived at the same time
teens.explanation: John 20:4 — "the other disciple did outrun Peter, and came first to the sepulchre."

teens.question: What did Jesus say to Mary Magdalene when she recognized Him?
teens.option_a: "Touch me not; for I am not yet ascended to my Father"
teens.option_b: "Go and tell my disciples I am alive"
teens.option_c: "Do not be afraid; it is I"
teens.explanation: John 20:17 — "Touch me not; for I am not yet ascended to my Father."

teens.question: What did Jesus breathe on the disciples and say?
teens.option_a: "Receive ye the Holy Ghost"
teens.option_b: "Peace be unto you"
teens.option_c: "Be filled with power"
teens.explanation: John 20:22 — "he breathed on them, and saith unto them, Receive ye the Holy Ghost."

teens.question: What did Thomas say when he finally saw Jesus?
teens.option_a: "My Lord and my God"
teens.option_b: "I believe; help my unbelief"
teens.option_c: "Truly you are the Son of God"
teens.explanation: John 20:28 — "Thomas answered and said unto him, My Lord and my God."

adults.question: What specific detail about the grave clothes did Peter see when he entered the tomb?
adults.option_a: The linen clothes were lying there, and the napkin that was about His head was wrapped together in a place by itself
adults.option_b: The clothes were scattered on the floor
adults.option_c: The clothes were folded neatly in a pile together
adults.option_d: The clothes were hanging on the wall of the tomb
adults.explanation: John 20:6-7 — "seeth the linen clothes lie, And the napkin, that was about his head, not lying with the linen clothes, but wrapped together in a place by itself."

adults.question: What did Mary Magdalene see inside the tomb?
adults.option_a: Two angels in white sitting where the body of Jesus had lain, one at the head and one at the feet
adults.option_b: A single angel standing at the entrance
adults.option_c: A bright light filling the empty space
adults.option_d: Three men in shining garments
adults.explanation: John 20:12 — "seeth two angels in white sitting, the one at the head, and the other at the feet, where the body of Jesus had lain."

adults.question: Who did Mary Magdalene initially think the risen Jesus was?
adults.option_a: The gardener
adults.option_b: An angel
adults.option_c: A Roman soldier
adults.option_d: One of the disciples
adults.explanation: John 20:15 — "She, supposing him to be the gardener."

adults.question: What words did Jesus say that caused Mary to recognize Him?
adults.option_a: He said her name: "Mary"
adults.option_b: He quoted a passage of Scripture
adults.option_c: He showed her His hands
adults.option_d: He reminded her of a past miracle
adults.explanation: John 20:16 — "Jesus saith unto her, Mary. She turned herself, and saith unto him, Rabboni."

adults.question: What authority did Jesus give the disciples regarding sins?
adults.option_a: "Whose soever sins ye remit, they are remitted unto them; and whose soever sins ye retain, they are retained"
adults.option_b: "You have authority to forgive all sins in my name"
adults.option_c: "Preach repentance and the forgiveness of sins"
adults.option_d: "Pray for sinners and I will hear your prayers"
adults.explanation: John 20:23 — "Whose soever sins ye remit, they are remitted unto them; and whose soever sins ye retain, they are retained."

adults.question: What specific proof did Thomas demand before he would believe?
adults.option_a: "Except I shall see in his hands the print of the nails, and put my finger into the print of the nails, and thrust my hand into his side, I will not believe"
adults.option_b: "Unless He appears to me alone and speaks my name"
adults.option_c: "Unless He performs a miracle before my eyes"
adults.option_d: "Unless Peter and John both testify under oath"
adults.explanation: John 20:25 — "Except I shall see in his hands the print of the nails, and put my finger into the print of the nails, and thrust my hand into his side, I will not believe."

adults.question: What did Jesus say to Thomas after Thomas believed?
adults.option_a: "Thomas, because thou hast seen me, thou hast believed: blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed"
adults.option_b: "Your faith has made you whole"
adults.option_c: "Go and tell the others what you have seen"
adults.option_d: "Do not doubt again, but always believe"
adults.explanation: John 20:29 — "Thomas, because thou hast seen me, thou hast believed: blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed."

adults.question: What does John state as the purpose of writing his Gospel?
adults.option_a: "These are written, that ye might believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God; and that believing ye might have life through his name"
adults.option_b: "These are written as a historical record for future generations"
adults.option_c: "These are written so that the church may have authority"
adults.option_d: "These are written to fulfill the words of the prophets"
adults.explanation: John 20:31 — "these are written, that ye might believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God; and that believing ye might have life through his name."

kids.question: How many fish did the disciples catch when Jesus told them to cast the net on the right side?
kids.explanation: John 21:11 — "an hundred and fifty and three" great fishes.

kids.question: What question did Jesus ask Peter three times?
kids.explanation: John 21:15-17 — Jesus asked Peter three times if he loved Him.

kids.question: What did Jesus tell Peter to do after Peter said he loved Him?
kids.explanation: John 21:15-17 — Jesus said "Feed my lambs," "Feed my sheep," and "Feed my sheep."

teens.question: Where did Jesus appear to the disciples in this chapter?
teens.option_a: At the Sea of Tiberias (Sea of Galilee)
teens.option_b: In the upper room in Jerusalem
teens.option_c: On the Mount of Olives
teens.explanation: John 21:1 — "Jesus shewed himself again to the disciples at the sea of Tiberias."

teens.question: What had the disciples been doing all night before Jesus appeared?
teens.option_a: Fishing — but they had caught nothing
teens.option_b: Praying in a house nearby
teens.option_c: Walking along the shore
teens.explanation: John 21:3 — "They went forth, and entered into a ship immediately; and that night they caught nothing."

teens.question: Which disciple jumped into the sea to swim to Jesus when he recognized Him?
teens.option_a: Peter
teens.option_b: John
teens.option_c: Thomas
teens.explanation: John 21:7 — "Simon Peter… did cast himself into the sea."

teens.question: Why do scholars think Jesus asked Peter "Lovest thou me?" three times?
teens.option_a: To restore Peter after his three denials
teens.option_b: Because Peter was hard of hearing
teens.option_c: To make sure the other disciples heard
teens.option_d: Because it was a Jewish legal custom
teens.explanation: John 21:15-17 — The three questions correspond to Peter's three denials in John 18.

teens.question: What did Jesus say about how Peter would die?
teens.option_a: He would stretch forth his hands and be carried where he did not wish to go (signifying crucifixion)
teens.option_b: He would die peacefully in old age
teens.option_c: He would be stoned for preaching
teens.explanation: John 21:18-19 — "thou shalt stretch forth thy hands, and another shall gird thee, and carry thee whither thou wouldest not. This spake he, signifying by what death he should glorify God."

adults.question: What was already prepared on shore when the disciples arrived with the fish?
adults.option_a: A fire of coals with fish laid on it and bread
adults.option_b: A feast prepared by angels
adults.option_c: A table set with cups and wine
adults.option_d: A tent with provisions inside
adults.explanation: John 21:9 — "they saw a fire of coals there, and fish laid thereon, and bread."

adults.question: Despite the great number of fish (153), what was notable about the net?
adults.option_a: It was not broken
adults.option_b: It was torn in many places
adults.option_c: It sank under the weight
adults.option_d: It had to be dragged by two boats
adults.explanation: John 21:11 — "drew the net to land full of great fishes, an hundred and fifty and three: and for all there were so many, yet was not the net broken."

adults.question: Which disciple first recognized that the figure on shore was Jesus?
adults.option_a: The disciple whom Jesus loved (John) said "It is the Lord"
adults.option_b: Peter recognized Him immediately
adults.option_c: Thomas pointed Him out
adults.option_d: Andrew said "It is the Master"
adults.explanation: John 21:7 — "Therefore that disciple whom Jesus loved saith unto Peter, It is the Lord."

adults.question: What distinction in Greek words for "love" is significant in the exchange between Jesus and Peter?
adults.option_a: Jesus first used "agapao" (selfless love); Peter responded with "phileo" (affection). The third time Jesus switched to "phileo"
adults.option_b: Jesus used "phileo" all three times and Peter used "agapao"
adults.option_c: Both used the same word "agapao" consistently
adults.option_d: Jesus used "eros" and Peter used "agapao"
adults.explanation: In the Greek text, Jesus asks with "agapas me" twice, then "phileis me" the third time. Peter answers "philo se" all three times.

adults.question: What did Peter ask Jesus about the beloved disciple?
adults.option_a: "Lord, and what shall this man do?" — Jesus replied "If I will that he tarry till I come, what is that to thee? follow thou me"
adults.option_b: "Will he also suffer for your name?"
adults.option_c: "Is he greater than I in your sight?"
adults.option_d: "Shall he lead the church after you go?"
adults.explanation: John 21:21-22 — "Lord, and what shall this man do? Jesus saith unto him, If I will that he tarry till I come, what is that to thee? follow thou me."

adults.question: What rumor spread among the brethren based on Jesus' words about the beloved disciple?
adults.option_a: That the beloved disciple would not die — but John clarifies Jesus did not say that
adults.option_b: That the beloved disciple would become the next high priest
adults.option_c: That the beloved disciple would write a Gospel
adults.option_d: That the beloved disciple would travel to Rome
adults.explanation: John 21:23 — "Then went this saying abroad among the brethren, that that disciple should not die: yet Jesus said not unto him, He shall not die."

adults.question: How does the final verse of John's Gospel describe the extent of Jesus' works?
adults.option_a: "If they should be written every one… even the world itself could not contain the books that should be written"
adults.option_b: "His works were so numerous that no man could count them"
adults.option_c: "The scrolls of the temple could not hold all His deeds"
adults.option_d: "A thousand scribes writing day and night could not finish"
adults.explanation: John 21:25 — "if they should be written every one, I suppose that even the world itself could not contain the books that should be written."

adults.question: How many times total had Jesus appeared to the disciples after the resurrection, according to John's count at the Sea of Tiberias?
adults.option_a: This was the third time Jesus showed Himself to the disciples after He was risen
adults.option_b: This was the second appearance to the disciples
adults.option_c: This was the fourth time Jesus appeared
adults.option_d: This was the fifth appearance recorded by John
adults.explanation: John 21:14 — "This is now the third time that Jesus shewed himself to his disciples, after that he was risen from the dead."
